translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: (And he prayed:) "O Lord, grant me a righteous son."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “My Lord! Give me a meritorious child.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: My Lord, give me one of the righteous.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: My Lord! Bestow on me a son who will be of the righteous.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "My Lord! Grant me (offspring) from the righteous."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “My Lord, give me one of the righteous.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Lord, grant me a righteous son.”

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "My Lord! Grant me (offspring) from the righteous."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: My Lord! Vouchsafe me of the righteous.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: My Lord, grant me a righteous (son)'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: My Lord, grant me [a child] from among the righteous."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Abraham prayed, "Lord, grant me a righteous son".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: My Lord! grant me of the doers of good deeds.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Lord, grant me a righteous son."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "O my Lord! Grant me a righteous (son)!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Remember when Abraham prayed: "O Lord, make this a city of peace, and preserve me and my progeny from worshipping idols:

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And remember when Ibrahim prayed, “O my Lord! Make this town (Mecca) a safe one, and safeguard me and my sons from worshipping idols.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And when Abraham said, 'My Lord, make this land secure, and turn me and my sons away from serving idols;

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And recall what time Ibrahim said: my Lord! make this City secure and keep me and my sons away from worshipping the idols.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And (remember) when Ibrahim (Abraham) said: "O my Lord! Make this city (Makkah) one of peace and security, and keep me and my sons away from worshipping idols.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Recall that Abraham said, “O my Lord, make this land peaceful, and keep me and my sons from worshiping idols.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And call to mind when Abraham prayed: "My Lord! Make this city secure, and keep me and my sons away from worshipping the idols.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And (remember) when Ibrahim said: "O my Lord! Make this city (Makkah) one of peace and security, and keep me and my sons away from worshipping idols.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And when Abraham said: My Lord! Make safe this territory, and preserve me and my sons from serving idols.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And when Abraham said: 'My Lord, make this a land secure. Turn me and my children away from worshipping idols.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And [mention, O Muhammad], when Abraham said, "My Lord, make this city [Makkah] secure and keep me and my sons away from worshipping idols.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: (Muhammad), consider when Abraham prayed, "Lord, make this (Mecca) a peaceful territory and save me and my offspring from worshipping idols.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And when Ibrahim said: My Lord! make this city secure, and save me and my sons from worshipping idols:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: [Remember] when Abraham said, "My Lord, make this a city of peace and help me and my children to keep away from worshipping idols.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Remember Abraham said: "O my Lord! make this city one of peace and security: and preserve me and my sons from worshipping idols.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Many a man have they led astray, O Lord. So he who follows me is truly of me; but as for him who disobeys me, surely You are forgiving and kind.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “O my Lord! The idols have led many people astray; so whoever followed me, is indeed mine; and whoever disobeyed me – then indeed You are Oft Forgiving, Most Merciful.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: my Lord, they have led astray many men. Then whoso follows me belongs to me; and whoso rebels against me, surely Thou art All-forgiving, All-compassionate.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: My Lord! they have sent astray many among mankind; whosoever followeth me, verily he is of me; and whosoever disobeyeth me, then verily Thou art Forgiving, Merciful.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "O my Lord! They have indeed led astray many among mankind. But whoso follows me, he verily is of me. And whoso disobeys me, - still You are indeed Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “My Lord, they have led many people astray. Whoever follows me belongs with me; and whoever disobeys me—You are Forgiving and Merciful.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: My Lord! They have caused many people to go astray. Now, if anyone follows my way, he is from me; and if anyone follows a way opposed to mine, then surely You are Ever-Forgiving, Most Merciful."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "O my Lord! They have indeed led astray many among mankind. But whoso follows me, he verily, is of me. And whoso disobeys me, still You are indeed Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: My Lord! Lo! they have led many of mankind astray. But whoso followeth me, he verily is of me. And whoso disobeyeth me - Still Thou art Forgiving, Merciful.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: My Lord they have led many people astray. Whosoever follows me belongs to me. Whosoever rebels against me, You are surely Forgiving, the MostMerciful.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: My Lord, indeed they have led astray many among the people. So whoever follows me - then he is of me; and whoever disobeys me - indeed, You are [yet] Forgiving and Merciful.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Lord, the idols have misled many people. Whoever follows me is my friend. As for those who disobey, You are certainly All-forgiving and All-merciful.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: My Lord! surely they have led many men astray; then whoever follows me, he is surely of me, and whoever disobeys me, Thou surely art Forgiving, Merciful:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: My Lord, they have led so many men astray! Anyone who follows me is with me, but if anyone turns against me, You are surely forgiving and merciful.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "O my Lord! they have indeed led astray many among mankind; He then who follows my (ways) is of me, and he that disobeys me,- but Thou art indeed Oft-forgiving, Most Merciful.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: So We gave him the good news of a clement son.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: We therefore gave him the glad tidings of an intelligent son.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Then We gave him the good tidings of a prudent boy;

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Wherefore We gave him the glad tidings of a boy gentle.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: So We gave him the glad tidings of a forbearing boy.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: So We gave him good news of a clement boy.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: (In response to this prayer) We gave him the good news of a prudent boy;

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: So, We gave him the glad tidings of a forbearing boy.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: So We gave him tidings of a gentle son.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And We gave him the glad tidings of a very gentle son (Ishmael).

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: So We gave him good tidings of a forbearing boy.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We gave him the glad news of the birth of a forbearing son.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: So We gave him the good news of a boy, possessing forbearance.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We gave him the good news that he would have a patient, forbearing son.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: So We gave him the good news of a boy ready to suffer and forbear.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: "Have no fear," they said. "We bring you news of a son full of wisdom."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: They said, “Do not fear – we convey to you the glad tidings of a knowledgeable boy.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: They said, 'Be not afraid; behold, we give thee good tidings of a cunning boy.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: They said: be not afraid; verily we bear thou the glad tidings of a boy knowing.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: They (the angels) said: "Do not be afraid! We give you glad tidings of a boy (son) possessing much knowledge and wisdom."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: They said, “Do not fear; we bring you good news of a boy endowed with knowledge.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: They said: "Do not feel afraid, for we give you the good news of a wise boy."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: They said: "Do not be afraid! We bring you the good news of a boy possessing much knowledge and wisdom."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: They said: Be not afraid! Lo! we bring thee good tidings of a boy possessing wisdom.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: 'Do not be afraid' they answered. 'We come to give you glad tidings of a knowledgeable child'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: [The angels] said, "Fear not. Indeed, we give you good tidings of a learned boy."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: They replied, "Do not be afraid. We have brought you the glad news of (the birth) of a learned son".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: They said: Be not afraid, surely we give you the good news of a boy, possessing knowledge.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: They said, "Do not be afraid. We come to you with good news. You shall have a son who shall be endowed with great knowledge."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: They said: "Fear not! We give thee glad tidings of a son endowed with wisdom."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: "You bring me the good news now," he said, "when old age has come upon me. What good news are you giving me then?"

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: He said, “Do you convey to me the glad tidings upon old age reaching me? So upon what do you convey glad tidings?” (* Prophet Ibrahim said this out of surprise.)

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: He said, 'What, do you give me good tidings, though old age has smitten me? Of what do you

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: He said: bear ye me glad tidings when old age hath touched me? of what then ye bear me glad tidings?

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: [Ibrahim (Abraham)] said: "Do you give me glad tidings (of a son) when old age has overtaken me? Of what then is your news?"

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He said, “Do you bring me good news, when old age has overtaken me? What good news do you bring?”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Abraham said: "What, do you give me this tiding though old age has smitten me? Just consider what tiding do you give me!"

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: He said: "Do you give me this good news while old age has overtaken me Of what then is your news about"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: He said: Bring ye me good tidings (of a son) when old age hath overtaken me? Of what then can ye bring good tidings?

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He said: 'What is this, do you bring me glad tidings even though I am old' Of what do you give me glad tidings'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: He said, "Have you given me good tidings although old age has come upon me? Then of what [wonder] do you inform?"

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Abraham asked, "Are you giving me the glad news of a son in my old age? What reason can you give for such glad news?"

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He said: Do you give me good news (of a son) when old age has come upon me?-- Of what then do you give me good news!

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: He said, "Do you bring me such news despite my old age? What kind of good news are you bringing me?"

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: He said: "Do ye give me glad tidings that old age has seized me? Of what, then, is your good news?"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: "We have given you the happy tidings of a truth," they replied. "So do not be one of those who despair."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: They said, “We have given you true tidings, therefore do not lose hope.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: give me good tidings?' They said, 'We give thee good tidings of truth. Be not of those that despair.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: They said: we bear tidings of a truth; be then thou not of the desponding.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: They (the angels) said: "We give you glad tidings in truth. So be not of the despairing ones."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: They said, “We bring you good news in truth, so do not despair.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: They said: "The good tiding we give you is of truth. Do not, therefore, be of those who despair."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: They said: "We give you good news in truth. So do not be of those who despair."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: They said: We bring thee good tidings in truth. So be not thou of the despairing.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: They replied: 'In truth we have given you glad tidings, do not be one of those who despair'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: They said, "We have given you good tidings in truth, so do not be of the despairing."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: They said, "We have given you the glad news for a true reason so do not despair".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: They said: We give you good news with truth, therefore be not of the despairing.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: They said, "We have, indeed, given you glad tidings in truth; do not therefore despair."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: They said: "We give thee glad tidings in truth: be not then in despair!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: I have settled some of my children, O Lord, in a barren valley near Your sacred House, so that, O our Lord, they may be constant in devotion. So put in the hearts of men some kindness for them, and provide fruits for them: They may haply be grateful.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “O our Lord! I have settled some of my descendants in a valley having no cultivation, near Your Sacred House – O our Lord! So that they may keep the prayer established, therefore incline some hearts of men towards them, and provide them fruits to eat – perhaps they may be thankful.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Our Lord, I have made some of my seed to dwell in a valley where is no sown land by Thy Holy House; Our Lord, let them perform the prayer, and make hearts of men yearn towards them, and provide them with fruits; haply they will be thankful.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Our Lord! verily have caused some of my progeny to dwell in a valley uncultivable near Thy Sacred House, Our Lord! in order that they may establish prayer; make Thou therefore the hearts of some of mankind to Yearn toward them, and provide them Thou With fruits, haply they may give thanks.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "O our Lord! I have made some of my offspring to dwell in an uncultivable valley by Your Sacred House (the Ka'bah at Makkah); in order, O our Lord, that they may perform As-Salat (Iqamat-as-Salat), so fill some hearts among men with love towards them, and (O Allah) provide them with fruits so that they may give thanks.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “Our Lord, I have settled some of my offspring in a valley of no vegetation, by Your Sacred House, our Lord, so that they may perform the prayers. So make the hearts of some people incline towards them, and provide them with fruits, that they may be thankful.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: "Our Lord! I have made some of my offspring settle in a barren valley near Your Sacred House! Our Lord! I did so that they may establish Prayer. So make the hearts of people affectionately inclined to them, and provide them with fruits for their sustenance that they may give thanks.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "O our Lord! I have made some of my offspring dwell in an uncultivable valley by Your Sacred House in order, O our Lord, that they may perform Salah. So fill some hearts among men with love towards them, and (O Allah) provide them with fruits so that they may give thanks.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Our Lord! Lo! I have settled some of my posterity in an uncultivable valley near unto Thy holy House, our Lord! that they may establish proper worship; so incline some hearts of men that they may yearn toward them, and provide Thou them with fruits in order that they may be thankful.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: 'Our Lord, I have settled some of my offspring in a barren valley near Your Holy House; our Lord, in order that they establish the prayer. Make the hearts of people yearn towards them, and provide them with fruits, in order that they are thankful.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Our Lord, I have settled some of my descendants in an uncultivated valley near Your sacred House, our Lord, that they may establish prayer. So make hearts among the people incline toward them and provide for them from the fruits that they might be grateful.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: "Lord, I have settled some of my offspring in a barren valley near your Sacred House so that they could be steadfast in prayer. Lord, fill the hearts of the people with love for them and produce fruits for their sustenance so that they may give thanks.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: O our Lord! surely I have settled a part of my offspring in a valley unproductive of fruit near Thy Sacred House, our Lord! that they may keep up prayer; therefore make the hearts of some people yearn towards them and provide them with fruits; haply they may be grateful:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: O Lord! I have settled some of my offspring in an uncultivable valley near Your Sacred House, Lord, so that they might establish their prayers. So, make people's hearts incline towards them and provide them with fruits so that they may be grateful.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "O our Lord! I have made some of my offspring to dwell in a valley without cultivation, by Thy Sacred House; in order, O our Lord, that they may establish regular Prayer: so fill the hearts of some among men with love towards them, and feed them with fruits: so that they may give thanks.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: O Lord, You have knowledge of what we hide and what we reveal, for nothing on the earth or in the skies is hidden from God.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “O our Lord! You know what we hide and what we disclose; and nothing is hidden from Allah, neither in the earth nor in the heavens.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Our Lord, Thou knowest what we keep secret and what we publish; from God nothing whatever is hidden in earth and heaven.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Our Lord! verily Thou knowest that which We conceal and that which We make known; and not of aught is concealed from Allah in the earth or in the heaven.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "O our Lord! Certainly, You know what we conceal and what we reveal. Nothing on the earth or in the heaven is hidden from Allah.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “Our Lord, You know what we conceal and what we reveal. And nothing is hidden from God, on earth or in the heaven.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Our Lord! Surely You know all that we conceal and all that we reveal, and nothing in the earth or in the heaven is hidden from Allah.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "O our Lord! Certainly, You know what we conceal and what we reveal. Nothing on the earth or in the heaven is hidden from Allah."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Our Lord! Lo! Thou knowest that which we hide and that which we proclaim. Nothing in the earth or in the heaven is hidden from Allah.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Our Lord, You have knowledge of all that we hide and all that we reveal; nothing in heaven or earth is hidden from Allah.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Our Lord, indeed You know what we conceal and what we declare, and nothing is hidden from Allah on the earth or in the heaven.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Lord, You know all that we conceal or reveal. Nothing in the heavens or the earth is hidden from God.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: O our Lord! Surely Thou knowest what we hide and what we make public, and nothing in the earth nor any thing in heaven is hidden from Allah:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Lord, You have knowledge of all that we hide and all that we reveal: nothing in heaven or on earth is hidden from God.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "O our Lord! truly Thou dost know what we conceal and what we reveal: for nothing whatever is hidden from Allah, whether on earth or in heaven.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: All praise be to God who bestowed on me Ishmael and Isaac in old age. Verily my Lord listens to prayer.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “All praise is to Allah Who gave me Ismail and Ishaq, in my old age; indeed my Lord is the Listener of Prayer.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Praise be to God, who has given me, though I am old, Ishmael and Isaac; surely my Lord hears the petition.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: All praise be unto Allah who hath bestowed upon me, despite old age lsma'il and ls-haq; verily my Lord is the Hearer of supplication.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "All the praises and thanks be to Allah, Who has given me in old age Isma'il (Ishmael) and Ishaque (Isaac). Verily! My Lord is indeed the All-Hearer of invocations.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “Praise be to God, Who has given me, in my old age, Ishmael and Isaac. My Lord is the Hearer of Prayers.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: All praise be to Allah Who, despite my old age, has given me Ishmael and Isaac. Surely my Lord hears all prayers.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "All praise is due to Allah, Who has given me in old age Isma'il and Ishaq. Verily, my Lord is indeed the All-Hearer of invocations."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Praise be to Allah Who hath given me, in my old age, Ishmael and Isaac! Lo! my Lord is indeed the Hearer of Prayer.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Praise be to Allah who has given me Ishmael and Isaac in my old age! Indeed, my Lord is the Hearer of the supplication.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Praise to Allah, who has granted to me in old age Ishmael and Isaac. Indeed, my Lord is the Hearer of supplication.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: It is only God who deserves all praise. I praise Him for His giving me my sons Ishmael and Isaac during my old age. My Lord, certainly, hears all prayers.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Praise be to Allah, Who has given me in old age Ismail and Ishaq; most surely my Lord is the Hearer of prayer:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Praise be to God who has bestowed upon me, despite my old age, Ishmael and Isaac. Surely my Lord is the hearer of prayer.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "Praise be to Allah, Who hath granted unto me in old age Isma'il and Isaac: for truly my Lord is He, the Hearer of Prayer!

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Grant, O Lord, that I and my offspring may remain constant in devotion. Grant, O Lord, my supplication.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “O my Lord! Maintain me as one who establishes prayer, and some of my descendants; O our Lord! and accept my prayer.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: My Lord, make me a performer of the prayer, and of my seed. Our Lord, and receive my petition.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: My Lord! make me establisher of prayer and also from my progeny, our Lord! and accept Thou my supplication.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "O my Lord! Make me one who performs As-Salat (Iqamat-as-Salat), and (also) from my offspring, our Lord! And accept my invocation.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “My Lord, make me one who performs the prayer, and from my offspring. Our Lord, accept my supplication.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: My Lord! Enable me and my offspring to establish Prayer, and do accept, our Lord, this prayer of mine.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "O my Lord! Make me one who performs Salah, and (also) from my offspring, our Lord! And accept my invocation."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: My Lord! Make me to establish proper worship, and some of my posterity (also); our Lord! and accept my prayer.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: My Lord, make me and my descendants establishers of prayer. Our Lord, accept my prayer.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: My Lord, make me an establisher of prayer, and [many] from my descendants. Our Lord, and accept my supplication.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Lord, make me and my offspring steadfast in prayer and accept our worship.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: My Lord! make me keep up prayer and from my offspring (too), O our Lord, and accept my prayer:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Lord, grant that I may keep up the prayer, and so may my offspring. My Lord, accept my prayer.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: O my Lord! make me one who establishes regular Prayer, and also (raise such) among my offspring O our Lord! and accept Thou my Prayer.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: O Lord, forgive me, my parents and the faithful on the Day the reckoning is done."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “O our Lord! And forgive me, and my parents, and all the Muslims on the day when the account will be established.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Our Lord, forgive Thou me and my parents, and the believers, upon the day when the reckoning shall come to pass.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Our Lord! forgive me and my parents and the believers on the Day whereon will be set up the reckoning.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "Our Lord! Forgive me and my parents, and (all) the believers on the Day when the reckoning will be established."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “Our Lord, forgive me, and my parents, and the believers, on the Day the Reckoning takes place.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Our Lord! Forgive me and my parents and the believers on the Day when the reckoning will take place."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "Our Lord! Forgive me and my parents, and (all) the believers on the Day when the reckoning will be established."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Our Lord! Forgive me and my parents and believers on the day when the account is cast.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Forgive me, our Lord, and forgive my parents and all the believers on the Day of Reckoning'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Our Lord, forgive me and my parents and the believers the Day the account is established."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Lord, on the Day of Judgment, forgive me and my parents and all the believers."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: O our Lord! grant me protection and my parents and the believers on the day when the reckoning shall come to pass!

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Forgive me, Lord, and forgive my parents and all the believers on the Day of Reckoning."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "O our Lord! cover (us) with Thy Forgiveness - me, my parents, and (all) Believers, on the Day that the Reckoning will be established!

translation.author: Dr. Muhammad Muhsin
translation.text: Narrated Ibn Abbas: The first lady to use a girdle was the mother of Ishmael. She used a girdle so that she might hide her tracks from Sarah. Abraham brought her and her son Ishmael while she was suckling him, to a place near the Ka'ba under a tree on the spot of Zam-zam, at the highest place in the mosque. During those days there was nobody in Mecca, nor was there any water So he made them sit over there and placed near them a leather bag containing some dates, and a small water-skin containing some water, and set out homeward. Ishmael's mother followed him saying, "O Abraham! Where are you going, leaving us in this valley where there is no person whose company we may enjoy, nor is there anything (to enjoy)?" She repeated that to him many times, but he did not look back at her Then she asked him, "Has Allah ordered you to do so?" He said, "Yes." She said, "Then He will not neglect us," and returned while Abraham proceeded onwards, and on reaching the Thaniya where they could not see him, he faced the Ka'ba, and raising both hands, invoked Allah saying the following prayers: 'O our Lord! I have made some of my offspring dwell in a valley without cultivation, by Your Sacred House (Kaba at Mecca) in order, O our Lord, that they may offer prayer perfectly. So fill some hearts among men with love towards them, and (O Allah) provide them with fruits, so that they may give thanks.' (14.37) Ishmael's mother went on suckling Ishmael and drinking from the water (she had). When the water in the water-skin had all been used up, she became thirsty and her child also became thirsty. She started looking at him (i.e. Ishmael) tossing in agony; She left him, for she could not endure looking at him, and found that the mountain of Safa was the nearest mountain to her on that land. She stood on it and started looking at the valley keenly so that she might see somebody, but she could not see anybody. Then she descended from Safa and when she reached the valley, she tucked up her robe and ran in the valley like a person in distress and trouble, till she crossed the valley and reached the Marwa mountain where she stood and started looking, expecting to see somebody, but she could not see anybody. She repeated that (running between Safa and Marwa) seven times." The Prophet said, "This is the source of the tradition of the walking of people between them (i.e. Safa and Marwa). When she reached the Marwa (for the last time) she heard a voice and she asked herself to be quiet and listened attentively. She heard the voice again and said, 'O, (whoever you may be)! You have made me hear your voice; have you got something to help me?" And behold! She saw an angel at the place of Zam-zam, digging the earth with his heel (or his wing), till water flowed from that place. She started to make something like a basin around it, using her hand in this way, and started filling her water-skin with water with her hands, and the water was flowing out after she had scooped some of it." The Prophet added, "May Allah bestow Mercy on Ishmael's mother! Had she let the Zam-zam (flow without trying to control it) (or had she not scooped from that water) (to fill her water-skin), Zam-zam would have been a stream flowing on the surface of the earth." The Prophet further added, "Then she drank (water) and suckled her child. The angel said to her, 'Don't be afraid of being neglected, for this is the House of Allah which will be built by this boy and his father, and Allah never neglects His people.' The House (i.e. Kaba) at that time was on a high place resembling a hillock, and when torrents came, they flowed to its right and left. She lived in that way till some people from the tribe of Jurhum or a family from Jurhum passed by her and her child, as they (i.e. the Jurhum people) were coming through the way of Kada'. They landed in the lower part of Mecca where they saw a bird that had the habit of flying around water and not leaving it. They said, 'This bird must be flying around water, though we know that there is no water in this valley.' They sent one or two messengers who discovered the source of water, and returned to inform them of the water. So, they all came (towards the water)." The Prophet added, "Ishmael's mother was sitting near the water. They asked her, 'Do you allow us to stay with you?" She replied, 'Yes, but you will have no right to possess the water.' They agreed to that." The Prophet further said, "Ishmael's mother was pleased with the whole situation as she used to love to enjoy the company of the people. So, they settled there, and later on they sent for their families who came and settled with them so that some families became permanent residents there. The child (i.e. Ishmael) grew up and learnt Arabic from them and (his virtues) caused them to love and admire him as he grew up, and when he reached the age of puberty they made him marry a woman from amongst them. After Ishmael's mother had died, Abraham came after Ishmael's marriage in order to see his family that he had left before, but he did not find Ishmael there. When he asked Ishmael's wife about him, she replied, 'He has gone in search of our livelihood.' Then he asked her about their way of living and their condition, and she replied, 'We are living in misery; we are living in hardship and destitution,' complaining to him. He said, 'When your husband returns, convey my salutation to him and tell him to change the threshold of the gate (of his house).' When Ishmael came, he seemed to have felt something unusual, so he asked his wife, 'Has anyone visited you?' She replied, 'Yes, an old man of so-and-so description came and asked me about you and I informed him, and he asked about our state of living, and I told him that we were living in a hardship and poverty.' On that Ishmael said, 'Did he advise you anything?' She replied, 'Yes, he told me to convey his salutation to you and to tell you to change the threshold of your gate.' Ishmael said, 'It was my father, and he has ordered me to divorce you. Go back to your family.' So, Ishmael divorced her and married another woman from amongst them (i.e. Jurhum). Then Abraham stayed away from them for a period as long as Allah wished and called on them again but did not find Ishmael. So he came to Ishmael's wife and asked her about Ishmael. She said, 'He has gone in search of our livelihood.' Abraham asked her, 'How are you getting on?' asking her about their sustenance and living. She replied, 'We are prosperous and well-off (i.e. we have everything in abundance).' Then she thanked Allah' Abraham said, 'What kind of food do you eat?' She said. 'Meat.' He said, 'What do you drink?' She said, 'Water." He said, "O Allah! Bless their meat and water." The Prophet added, "At that time they did not have grain, and if they had grain, he would have also invoked Allah to bless it." The Prophet added, "If somebody has only these two things as his sustenance, his health and disposition will be badly affected, unless he lives in Mecca." The Prophet added," Then Abraham said Ishmael's wife, "When your husband comes, give my regards to him and tell him that he should keep firm the threshold of his gate.' When Ishmael came back, he asked his wife, 'Did anyone call on you?' She replied, 'Yes, a good-looking old man came to me,' so she praised him and added. 'He asked about you, and I informed him, and he asked about our livelihood and I told him that we were in a good condition.' Ishmael asked her, 'Did he give you any piece of advice?' She said, 'Yes, he told me to give his regards to you and ordered that you should keep firm the threshold of your gate.' On that Ishmael said, 'It was my father, and you are the threshold (of the gate). He has ordered me to keep you with me.' Then Abraham stayed away from them for a period as long as Allah wished, and called on them afterwards. He saw Ishmael under a tree near Zamzam, sharpening his arrows. When he saw Abraham, he rose up to welcome him (and they greeted each other as a father does with his son or a son does with his father). Abraham said, 'O Ishmael! Allah has given me an order.' Ishmael said, 'Do what your Lord has ordered you to do.' Abraham asked, 'Will you help me?' Ishmael said, 'I will help you.' Abraham said, Allah has ordered me to build a house here,' pointing to a hillock higher than the land surrounding it." The Prophet added, "Then they raised the foundations of the House (i.e. the Ka'ba). Ishmael brought the stones and Abraham was building, and when the walls became high, Ishmael brought this stone and put it for Abraham who stood over it and carried on building, while Ishmael was handing him the stones, and both of them were saying, 'O our Lord! Accept (this service) from us, Verily, You are the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.' The Prophet added, "Then both of them went on building and going round the Ka'ba saying: O our Lord ! Accept (this service) from us, Verily, You are the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing." (2.127)

translation.author: Dr. Muhammad Muhsin
translation.text: Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: The Prophet said, "May Allah bestow His Mercy on the mother of Ishmael! Had she not hastened (to fill her water-skin with water from the Zam-zam well). Zam-zam would have been a stream flowing on the surface of the earth." Ibn 'Abbas further added, "(The Prophet) Abraham brought Ishmael and his mother (to Mecca) and she was suckling Ishmael and she had a water-skin with her.'

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: When he was old enough to go about with him, he said: "O my son, I dreamt that I was sacrificing you. Consider, what you think?" He replied: "Father, do as you are commanded. If God pleases you will find me firm."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And when he became capable of working with him, Ibrahim said, “O my son, I dreamt that I am sacrificing you – therefore now consider what is your opinion”; he said, “O my father! Do what you are commanded! Allah willing, you will soon find me patiently enduring!”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and when he had reached the age of running with him, he said, 'My son, I see in a dream that I shall sacrifice thee; consider, what thinkest thou?' He said, 'My father, do as thou art bidden; thou shalt find me, God willing, one of the steadfast.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And when he attained the age of, running with him, he said: O my son! verily I have seen in a dream that I am slaughtering thee; so look, what considerest thou? He said: O my father! do that which thou art commanded; thou shalt find me, Allah willing, of the patients.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And, when he (his son) was old enough to walk with him, he said: "O my son! I have seen in a dream that I am slaughtering you (offer you in sacrifice to Allah), so look what you think!" He said: "O my father! Do that which you are commanded, Insha' Allah (if Allah will), you shall find me of As-Sabirin (the patient ones, etc.)."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Then, when he was old enough to accompany him, he said, “O My son, I see in a dream that I am sacrificing you; see what you think.” He said, “O my Father, do as you are commanded; you will find me, God willing, one of the steadfast.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: and when he was old enough to go about and work with him, (one day) Abraham said to him: “My son, I see in my dream that I am slaughtering you. So consider (and tell me) what you think.” He said: “Do as you are bidden. You will find me, if Allah so wills, among the steadfast.”

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And, when he (his son) was old enough to walk with him, he said: "O my son! I have seen in a dream that I am slaughtering you. So look what you think!" He said: "O my father! Do that which you are commanded, if Allah wills, you shall find me of the patient."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And when (his son) was old enough to walk with him, (Abraham) said: O my dear son, I have seen in a dream that I must sacrifice thee. So look, what thinkest thou? He said: O my father! Do that which thou art commanded. Allah willing, thou shalt find me of the steadfast.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And when he reached the age of traveling with him, he said: 'My son, while I was sleeping I saw that I shall slaughter (sacrifice) you, tell me what is your opinion' He replied: 'Father, do as you are ordered (by Allah). Allah willing, you shall find me one of those who are steadfast'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And when he reached with him [the age of] exertion, he said, "O my son, indeed I have seen in a dream that I [must] sacrifice you, so see what you think." He said, "O my father, do as you are commanded. You will find me, if Allah wills, of the steadfast."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: When his son was old enough to work with him, he said, "My son, I have had a dream that I must sacrifice you. What do you think of this?" He replied, "Father, fulfill whatever you are commanded to do and you will find me patient, by the will of God".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And when he attained to working with him, he said: O my son! surely I have seen in a dream that I should sacrifice you; consider then what you see. He said: O my father! do what you are commanded; if Allah please, you will find me of the patient ones.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: And when he reached the age when he could work with him, he said, "O my son, I have seen in a dream that I am sacrificing you. So tell me what you think of it!" He replied, "O my father, do as you are commanded; and God willing, you will find me steadfast."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Then, when (the son) reached (the age of) (serious) work with him, he said: "O my son! I see in vision that I offer thee in sacrifice: Now see what is thy view!" (The son) said: "O my father! Do as thou art commanded: thou will find me, if Allah so wills one practising Patience and Constancy!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: When they submitted to the will of God, and (Abraham) laid (his son) down prostrate on his temple,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Then (remember) when they both submitted to Allah’s command, and Ibrahim lay his son facing downwards. (The knife did not hurt Ismail)

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: When they had surrendered, and he flung him upon his brow,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Then when the twain had submitted themselves and he had prostrated him upon his temple.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Then, when they had both submitted themselves (to the Will of Allah), and he had laid him prostrate on his forehead (or on the side of his forehead for slaughtering);

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Then, when they had submitted, and he put his forehead down.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: When both surrendered (to Allah's command) and Abraham flung the son down on his forehead,

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Then, when they had both submitted themselves, and he had laid him prostrate on his forehead;

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Then, when they had both surrendered (to Allah), and he had flung him down upon his face,

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And when they had both submitted, and his son had laid down prostrate upon his forehead,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And when they had both submitted and he put him down upon his forehead,

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: When they both agreed and Abraham had lain down his son on the side of his face (for slaughtering),

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: So when they both submitted and he threw him down upon his forehead,

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: When they had both submitted to God, and he had laid his son down on his face,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: So when they had both submitted their wills (to Allah), and he had laid him prostrate on his forehead (for sacrifice),

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: We called out: "O Abraham,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We called out to him, “O Ibrahim!”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: We called unto him, 'Abraham,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: We cried Unto him: O Ibrahim

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And We called out to him: "O Abraham!

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: We called out to him, “O Abraham!

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: We cried out: “O Abraham,

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: We called out to him: "O Ibrahim!"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: We called unto him: O Abraham!

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We called to him, saying: 'O Abraham,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: We called to him, "O Abraham,

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We called to him, "Abraham,

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And We called out to him saying: O Ibrahim!

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We called out to him, "Abraham,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: We called out to him "O Abraham!

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: You have fulfilled your dream." Thus do We reward the good.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “You have indeed made the dream come true”; and this is how We reward the virtuous.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: thou hast confirmed the vision; even so We recompense the good-doers.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Of a surety thou hast fulfilled the vision. Verily We! thus We recompense the well-doers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: You have fulfilled the dream (vision)!" Verily! Thus do We reward the Muhsinun (good-doers - see V. 2:112).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: You have fulfilled the vision.” Thus We reward the doers of good.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: you have indeed fulfilled your dream. Thus do We reward the good-doers.”

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "You have fulfilled the dream!" Verily, thus do We reward the doers of good.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Thou hast already fulfilled the vision. Lo! thus do We reward the good.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: you have confirmed your vision' As such We recompense the gooddoers.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: You have fulfilled the vision." Indeed, We thus reward the doers of good.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: you have fulfilled what you were commanded to do in your dream." Thus do We reward the righteous ones.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: You have indeed shown the truth of the vision; surely thus do We reward the doers of good:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: you have fulfilled the dream." It is thus indeed that We reward those who do good,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "Thou hast already fulfilled the vision!" - thus indeed do We reward those who do right.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: That was indeed a trying test.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Indeed this was a clear test.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: This is indeed the manifest trial.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Verily that! that was a trial manifest.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Verily, that indeed was a manifest trial

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: This was certainly an evident test.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: This was indeed a plain trial.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Verily, that indeed was a manifest trial.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Lo! that verily was a clear test.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: That was indeed a clear trial.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Indeed, this was the clear trial.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: It was certainly an open trial.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Most surely this is a manifest trial.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: that surely was a manifest trial,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: For this was obviously a trial-

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: So We ransomed him for a great sacrifice,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We rescued him in exchange of a great sacrifice. (The sacrifice of Ibrahim and Ismail – peace be upon them – is commemorated every year on 10, 11 and 12 Zil Haj).

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And We ransomed him with a mighty sacrifice,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And We ransomed him with a mighty victim.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And We ransomed him with a great sacrifice (i.e. a ram);

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And We redeemed him with a great sacrifice.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And We ransomed him with a mighty sacrifice,

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And We ransomed him with a great sacrifice;

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Then We ransomed him with a tremendous victim.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: So, We ransomed him with a mighty sacrifice,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We ransomed him with a great sacrifice,

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We ransomed his son with a great sacrifice

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And We ransomed him with a Feat sacrifice.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We ransomed him with a great sacrifice,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And We ransomed him with a momentous sacrifice:

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And left (his hallowed memory) for posterity.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We kept his praise among the latter generations.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and left for him among the later folk

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And We left for him among the posterity:

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And We left for him (a goodly remembrance) among generations (to come) in later times.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And We left with him for later generations.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: and We preserved for him a good name among posterity.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And We left for him among the later generations.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And We left for him among the later folk (the salutation):

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: and We let it (the beautiful praise) remain upon him in the latter (generations),

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We left for him [favorable mention] among later generations:

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: and perpetuated his praise in later generations.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And We perpetuated (praise) to him among the later generations.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: and left him thus to be succeeded by a group [of followers] among later generations:

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And We left (this blessing) for him among generations (to come) in later times:

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Peace be on Abraham.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Peace be upon Ibrahim!

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: 'Peace be upon Abraham!'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Peace be Unto Ibrahim:

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Salamun (peace) be upon Ibrahim (Abraham)!"

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Peace be upon Abraham.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Peace be upon Abraham.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "Salam (peace!) be upon Ibrahim!"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Peace be unto Abraham!

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: 'Peace be upon Abraham'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: "Peace upon Abraham."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Peace be with Abraham.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Peace be on Ibrahim.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: "Peace and salutation to Abraham!"

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "Peace and salutation to Abraham!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: That is how We reward those who do good.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: This is how We reward the virtuous.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Even so We recompense the good-doers;

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Verily We! thus We compense the well-doers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Thus indeed do We reward the Muhsinun (good-doers - see V. 2:112).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Thus We reward the doers of good.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Thus do We reward the good-doers.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Thus indeed do We reward the doers of good.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Thus do We reward the good.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: As such We recompense the gooddoers.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Indeed, We thus reward the doers of good.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Thus, do We reward the righteous ones.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Thus do We reward the doers of good.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: That is how We recompense the righteous:

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Thus indeed do We reward those who do right.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: He is truly among Our faithful creatures.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: He is indeed one of Our high ranking, firmly believing bondmen.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: he was among Our believing servants.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Verily he was one of Our believing bondmen.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Verily, he was one of Our believing slaves.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He was one of Our believing servants.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Surely he was one of Our believing servants.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Verily, he was one of Our believing servants.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Lo! he is one of Our believing slaves.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He was one of Our believing worshipers.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Indeed, he was of Our believing servants.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: He was one of Our believing servants.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Surely he was one of Our believing servants.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: truly, he was one of Our faithful servants.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: For he was one of our believing Servants.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Remember, when his Lord tried Abraham by a number of commands which he fulfilled, God said to him: "I will make you a leader among men." And when Abraham asked: "From my progeny too?" the Lord said: "My pledge does not include transgressors."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And (remember) when Ibrahim’s (Abraham’s) Lord tested him in some matters and he fulfilled them; He said, “I am going to appoint you as a leader for mankind”; invoked Ibrahim, “And of my offspring”; He said, “My covenant does not include the unjust (wrong-doers).”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And when his Lord tested Abraham with certain words, and he fulfilled them. He said, 'Behold, I make you a leader for the people.' Said he, 'And of my seed?' He said 'My covenant shall not reach the evildoers.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And recall what time his Lord Proved Ibrahim with certain words then he performed them. He said: verily I am going to make thee a leader unto mankind. Ibrahim said: and also of my progeny? Allah said: My covenant shall not reach the wrong-doers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And (remember) when the Lord of Ibrahim (Abraham) [i.e., Allah] tried him with (certain) Commands, which he fulfilled. He (Allah) said (to him), "Verily, I am going to make you a leader (Prophet) of mankind." [Ibrahim (Abraham)] said, "And of my offspring (to make leaders)." (Allah) said, "My Covenant (Prophethood, etc.) includes not Zalimun (polytheists and wrong-doers)."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And when his Lord tested Abraham with certain words, and he fulfilled them. He said, “I am making you a leader of humanity.” He said, “And my descendants?” He said, “My pledge does not include the wrongdoers.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Recall to mind that when his Lord put Abraham to test in certain things and he fulfilled all of them, He said, "I am going to make you the leader of mankind." Abraham humbly asked, "Does this promise apply to my descendants also?" He replied, "My promise does not apply to the transgressors."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And (remember) when the Lord of Ibrahim (Abraham) tried him with (certain) commands, which he fulfilled. He (Allah) said (to him), "Verily, I am going to make you an Imam (a leader) for mankind (to follow you)." (Ibrahim) said, "And of my offspring (to make leaders)." (Allah) said, "My covenant (prophethood) includes not Zalimin (polytheists and wrongdoers)."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And (remember) when his Lord tried Abraham with (His) commands, and he fulfilled them, He said: Lo! I have appointed thee a leader for mankind. (Abraham) said: And of my offspring (will there be leaders)? He said: My covenant includeth not wrong-doers.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And when Abraham was tested by His Lord with certain words and he fulfilled them, He said: 'I have appointed you as a leader for the nation' (Abraham) asked: 'And of my descendants' 'My covenant' said He, 'the harmdoers shall not receive it'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And [mention, O Muhammad], when Abraham was tried by his Lord with commands and he fulfilled them. [Allah] said, "Indeed, I will make you a leader for the people." [Abraham] said, "And of my descendants?" [Allah] said, "My covenant does not include the wrongdoers."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: When his Lord tested Abraham's faith, (by His words) and he satisfied the test, He said, "I am appointing you as the leader of mankind." Abraham asked, "Will this leadership also continue through my descendants?" The Lord replied, "The unjust do not have the right to exercise My authority."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And when his Lord tried Ibrahim with certain words, he fulfilled them. He said: Surely I will make you an Imam of men. Ibrahim said: And of my offspring? My covenant does not include the unjust, said He.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: When his Lord tested Abraham with certain commands and he fulfilled them, He said, "I will make you a leader of men." Abraham asked, "And what of my descendants?" He answered, "My covenant does not extend to the transgressors."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And remember that Abraham was tried by his Lord with certain commands, which he fulfilled: He said: "I will make thee an Imam to the Nations." He pleaded: "And also (Imams) from my offspring!" He answered: "But My Promise is not within the reach of evil-doers."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Remember, We made the House (of Ka'bah) a place of congregation and safe retreat, and said: "Make the spot where Abraham stood the place of worship;" and enjoined upon Abraham and Ishmael to keep Our House immaculate for those who shall walk around it and stay in it for contemplation and prayer, and for bowing in adoration.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And remember when We made this House (at Mecca) a recourse for mankind and a sanctuary; and take the place where Ibrahim stood, as your place of prayer; and We imposed a duty upon Ibrahim and Ismail (Ishmael), to fully purify My house for those who go around it, and those who stay in it (for worship), and those who bow down and prostrate themselves.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And when We appointed the House to be a place of visitation for the people, and a sanctuary, and: 'Take to yourselves Abraham's station for a place of prayer.' And We made covenant with Abraham and Ishmael: 'Purify My House for those that shall go about it and those that cleave to it, to those who bow and prostrate themselves.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And recall what time We appointed the House a resort unto man kind and a place of security and said: take the station of Ibrahim for a place of prayer. And We covenanted with Ibrahim and Ismai'l, saying: purify ye twain My Houses for those who shall circumambulate it and those who shall stay, and those who shall bow down and prostrate themselves.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And (remember) when We made the House (the Ka'bah at Makkah) a place of resort for mankind and a place of safety. And take you (people) the Maqam (place) of Ibrahim (Abraham) [or the stone on which Ibrahim (Abraham) stood while he was building the Ka'bah] as a place of prayer (for some of your prayers, e.g. two Rak'at after the Tawaf of the Ka'bah at Makkah), and We commanded Ibrahim (Abraham) and Isma'il (Ishmael) that they should purify My House (the Ka'bah at Makkah) for those who are circumambulating it, or staying (I'tikaf), or bowing or prostrating themselves (there, in prayer).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And We made the House a focal point for the people, and a sanctuary. Use the shrine of Abraham as a place of prayer. And We commissioned Abraham and Ishmael, “Sanctify My House for those who circle around it, and those who seclude themselves in it, and those who kneel and prostrate.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And remember that We made this House (the Ka'bah) the centre and sanctuary for mankind and enjoined the people to dedicate to worship the place where Abraham used to stand for prayer, and We urged Abraham and Ismail to keep My House pure for those who would go round it and those who would retire to it for devotion and prayer and for those who would bow down and prostrate themselves there in worhsip.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And (remember) when We made the House (the Ka`bah at Makkah) a place of resort for mankind and a place of safety. And take you (people) the Maqam (place) of Ibrahim as a place of prayer. And We commanded Ibrahim (Abraham) and Isma`il (Ishmael) that they should purify My House (the Ka`bah at Makkah) for those who are circumambulating it, or staying (I`tikaf), or bowing or prostrating themselves (there, in prayer).

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And when We made the House (at Makka) a resort for mankind and sanctuary, (saying): Take as your place of worship the place where Abraham stood (to pray). And We imposed a duty upon Abraham and Ishmael, (saying): Purify My house for those who go around and those who meditate therein and those who bow down and prostrate themselves (in worship).

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And when We made the House (Ka'bah) a visitation and a sanctuary for the people (saying:) 'Make the place where Abraham stood a place of prayer. 'And We made a covenant with Abraham and Ishmael: 'Purify My House for those who circumambulate around it, and those who cleave to it, to those who bow and prostrate'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And [mention] when We made the House a place of return for the people and [a place of] security. And take, [O believers], from the standing place of Abraham a place of prayer. And We charged Abraham and Ishmael, [saying], "Purify My House for those who perform Tawaf and those who are staying [there] for worship and those who bow and prostrate [in prayer]."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We made the house (in Mecca) as a place of refuge and sanctuary for men. Adopt the place where Abraham stood as a place for prayer. We advised Abraham and Ishmael to keep My house clean for the pilgrims, the worshippers and for those who bow down and prostrate themselves in worship.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And when We made the House a pilgrimage for men and a (place of) security, and: Appoint for yourselves a place of prayer on the standing-place of Ibrahim. And We enjoined Ibrahim and Ismail saying: Purify My House for those who visit (it) and those who abide (in it) for devotion and those who bow down (and) those who prostrate themselves.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: And We made the House [the Ka'bah] a place of assembly and a sanctuary for mankind, [saying], "Make the place where Abraham stood a place of worship." We commanded Abraham and Ishmael, "Purify My House for those who walk round it, those who stay there for devotion, and those who bow down and prostrate themselves."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Remember We made the House a place of assembly for men and a place of safety; and take ye the station of Abraham as a place of prayer; and We covenanted with Abraham and Isma'il, that they should sanctify My House for those who compass it round, or use it as a retreat, or bow, or prostrate themselves (therein in prayer).

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And when Abraham said: "O Lord, make this a city of peace, and give those of its citizens who believe in God and the Last Day fruits for food," He answered: "To those will I also give a little who believe not, for a time, then drag them to Hell, a dreadful destination!"

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And (remember) when Ibrahim prayed, “My Lord! Make this city a place of security and bestow upon its people various fruits as providence – for those among them who believe in Allah and the Last Day (of Resurrection)”; He answered, “And whoever disbelieves, I shall provide him also some subsistence and then compel him towards the punishment of fire (hell); and that is a wretched place to return.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And when Abraham said, 'My Lord, make this a land secure, and provide its people with fruits, such of them as believe in God and the Last Day.' He said, 'And whoso disbelieves, to him I shall give enjoyment a little, then I shall compel him to the chastisement of the Fire -- how evil a homecoming!'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And recall what time Ibrahim said: my Lord! make this city a place of security, and provide the People thereof with fruits, - such of them as will believe in Allah and the last Day. Allah said: and whosoever will disbelieve, him also shall give enjoyment for a while; thereafter I shall drive him to the torment of the Fire, - an ill abode!

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And (remember) when Ibrahim (Abraham) said, "My Lord, make this city (Makkah) a place of security and provide its people with fruits, such of them as believe in Allah and the Last Day." He (Allah) answered: "As for him who disbelieves, I shall leave him in contentment for a while, then I shall compel him to the torment of the Fire, and worst indeed is that destination!"

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: When Abraham said, “O My Lord, make this a peaceful land, and provide its people with fruits—whoever of them believes in God and the Last Day.” He said, “And whoever disbelieves, I will give him a little enjoyment, then I will consign him to the punishment of the Fire; how miserable the destiny!”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And remember that Abraham prayed, "Lord, make this city a city of peace and security, and provide with every kind of fruit those of its people, who believe in Allah and the Last Day'. Allah answered, "As for the disbelievers, I will also provide them with the necessities of life in this world, though in the Next World I will drag them to the torment of Hell, and that is the worst abode."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And (remember) when Ibrahim said, "My Lord, make this city (Makkah) a place of security and provide its people with fruits, such of them as believe in Allah and the Last Day." He (Allah) answered: "As for him who disbelieves, I shall leave him in contentment for a while, then I shall compel him to the torment of the Fire, and worst indeed is that destination!"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And when Abraham prayed: My Lord! Make this a region of security and bestow upon its people fruits, such of them as believe in Allah and the Last Day, He answered: As for him who disbelieveth, I shall leave him in contentment for a while, then I shall compel him to the doom of Fire - a hapless journey's end!

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And when Abraham said: 'My Lord, make this country safe and provide its inhabitants whosoever of them believes in Allah and the Last Day with fruits' He said: 'And whosoever disbelieves, to him I shall give enjoyment for a while. Then I shall compel him to the punishment of the Fire, how evil an arrival'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And [mention] when Abraham said, "My Lord, make this a secure city and provide its people with fruits - whoever of them believes in Allah and the Last Day." [Allah] said. "And whoever disbelieves - I will grant him enjoyment for a little; then I will force him to the punishment of the Fire, and wretched is the destination."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: When Abraham prayed to the Lord saying, "Lord, make this town a place of security and provide those in the town who believe in God and the Day of Judgement, with plenty," God replied, "I shall allow those who hide the truth to enjoy themselves for a while. Then I shall drive them into the torment of hell fire, a terrible destination!"

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And when Ibrahim said: My Lord, make it a secure town and provide its people with fruits, such of them as believe in Allah and the last day. He said: And whoever disbelieves, I will grant him enjoyment for a short while, then I will drive him to the chastisement of the fire; and it is an evil destination.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: And when Abraham prayed, "My Lord, make this city a city of peace, and provide its inhabitants with fruits, such of them as believe in God and the Last Day." God said, "As for those who deny the truth, I will grant them benefits for a short while and then subject them to the punishment of the Fire -- an evil destination."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And remember Abraham said: "My Lord, make this a City of Peace, and feed its people with fruits,-such of them as believe in Allah and the Last Day." He said: "(Yea), and such as reject Faith,-for a while will I grant them their pleasure, but will soon drive them to the torment of Fire,- an evil destination (indeed)!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And when Abraham was raising the plinth of the House with Ishmael, (he prayed): "Accept this from us, O Lord, for You hear and know everything;

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And (remember) when Ibrahim was raising the foundations of the House, along with Ismail; (saying), “Our Lord! Accept it from us; indeed You only are the All Hearing, the All Knowing.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And when Abraham, and Ishmael with him, raised up the foundations of the House: 'Our Lord, receive this from us; Thou art the All-hearing, the All-knowing;

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And recall what time Ibrahim was raising the foundation of the House and also Ismai'l, praying: our Lord! accept of us; verily Thou! Thou art the Hearer, the Knower!

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And (remember) when Ibrahim (Abraham) and (his son) Isma'il (Ishmael) were raising the foundations of the House (the Ka'bah at Makkah), (saying), "Our Lord! Accept (this service) from us. Verily! You are the All-Hearer, the All-Knower."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: As Abraham raises the foundations of the House, together with Ishmael, “Our Lord, accept it from us, You are the Hearer, the Knower.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And remember that when Abraham and Ishmael were raising the walls of the House, they prayed, "Lord, accept this service from us; You are All-Hearing and All-Knowing.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And (remember) when Ibrahim and (his son) Isma`il were raising the foundations of the House (the Ka`bah at Makkah), (saying), "Our Lord! Accept (this service) from us. Verily, You are the Hearer, the Knower."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And when Abraham and Ishmael were raising the foundations of the House, (Abraham prayed): Our Lord! Accept from us (this duty). Lo! Thou, only Thou, art the Hearer, the Knower.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And when Abraham and Ishmael raised the foundations of the House (supplicating): 'O our Lord, accept this from us, You are the Hearer, the Knower.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And [mention] when Abraham was raising the foundations of the House and [with him] Ishmael, [saying], "Our Lord, accept [this] from us. Indeed You are the Hearing, the Knowing.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: While Abraham and Ishmael were raising the foundation of the house, they prayed, "Lord, accept our labor. You are All-hearing and All-knowing.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And when Ibrahim and Ismail raised the foundations of the House: Our Lord! accept from us; surely Thou art the Hearing, the Knowing:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: And when Abraham and Ishmael were laying the foundations of the House, [they prayed], "Our Lord, accept this from us; for You are All Hearing, All Knowing.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And remember Abraham and Isma'il raised the foundations of the House (With this prayer): "Our Lord! Accept (this service) from us: For Thou art the All-Hearing, the All-knowing.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And make us submit, O Lord, to Your will, and our progeny a people submissive to You. Teach us the way of worship and forgive our trespasses, for You are compassionate and merciful;

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “Our Lord! And make us submissive towards you and from our offspring a nation obedient to You – and show us the ways of our worship, and incline towards us with Your mercy; indeed You only are the Most Acceptor of Repentance, the Most Merciful.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and, our Lord, make us submissive to Thee, and of our seed a nation submissive to Thee; and show us our holy rites, and turn towards us; surely Thou turnest, and art All-compassionate;

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Our Lord! make us twain submissive unto Thee, and of our progeny community submissive unto Thee, and show us our rites, and relent toward us! verily Thou! Thou art the Relentant, the Merciful!

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "Our Lord! And make us submissive unto You and of our offspring a nation submissive unto You, and show us our Manasik (all the ceremonies of pilgrimage - Hajj and 'Umrah, etc.), and accept our repentance. Truly, You are the One Who accepts repentance, the Most Merciful.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Our Lord, and make us submissive to You, and from our descendants a community submissive to You. And show us our rites, and accept our repentance. You are the Acceptor of Repentance, the Merciful.

translation.author: Maududi
