translation.text: Lord, make us Your Muslims (submissive servants) and also raise from our offspring a community which should be Muslim (submissive to Your Will). Show us the ways of Your worship and forbear our shortcomings: You are Forgiving and Merciful.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "Our Lord! And make us submissive unto You and of our offspring a nation submissive unto You, and show us our Manasik, and accept our repentance. Truly, You are the One Who accepts repentance, the Most Merciful.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Our Lord! And make us submissive unto Thee and of our seed a nation submissive unto Thee, and show us our ways of worship, and relent toward us. Lo! Thou, only Thou, art the Relenting, the Merciful.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Our Lord, make us both submissive to You, and of our descendants a submissive nation to You. Show us our rites and accept us; You are the Receiver of Repentance, the Merciful.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Our Lord, and make us Muslims [in submission] to You and from our descendants a Muslim nation [in submission] to You. And show us our rites and accept our repentance. Indeed, You are the Accepting of repentance, the Merciful.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Lord, make us good Muslims (one who submits himself to God) and from our descendants make a good Muslim nation. Teach us the rules of worship and accept our repentance; You are All-forgiving and All-merciful.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Our Lord! and make us both submissive to Thee and (raise) from our offspring a nation submitting to Thee, and show us our ways of devotion and turn to us (mercifully), surely Thou art the Oft-returning (to mercy), the Merciful.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Lord, make us submissive to You; make of our descendants a nation that will submit to You. Teach us our rites of worship and turn to us with mercy; You are the Forgiving One and the Merciful.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "Our Lord! make of us Muslims, bowing to Thy (Will), and of our progeny a people Muslim, bowing to Thy (will); and show us our place for the celebration of (due) rites; and turn unto us (in Mercy); for Thou art the Oft-Returning, Most Merciful.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And send to them, O Lord, an apostle from among them to impart Your messages to them, and teach them the Book and the wisdom, and correct them in every way; for indeed You are mighty and wise.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “Our Lord! And send towards them a Noble Messenger, from amongst them, to recite to them Your verses, and to instruct them in Your Book and sound wisdom*, and to fully purify them; indeed You only are the Almighty, the Wise.” (The traditions of the Holy Prophet – sunnah and hadith – are called wisdom.)

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and, our Lord, do Thou send among them a Messenger, one of them, who shall recite to them Thy signs, and teach them the Book and the Wisdom, and purify them; Thou art the All-mighty, the All-wise.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Our Lord! raise up unto them an apostle from among them, who shall recite unto them Thy revelations, and shall teach them the Book and wisdom, and shall cleanse them. Verily Thou! Thou art the Mighty, the Wise.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "Our Lord! Send amongst them a Messenger of their own (and indeed Allah answered their invocation by sending Muhammad Peace be upon him), who shall recite unto them Your Verses and instruct them in the Book (this Quran) and Al-Hikmah (full knowledge of the Islamic laws and jurisprudence or wisdom or Prophethood, etc.), and sanctify them. Verily! You are the All-Mighty, the All-Wise."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Our Lord, and raise up among them a messenger, of themselves, who will recite to them Your revelations, and teach them the Book and wisdom, and purify them. You are the Almighty, the Wise.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Lord, raise up from among them a Messenger who shall recite Your Revelations to them and teach them the Book and Wisdom and purify their lives. You art All-Powerful and AllWise".

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "Our Lord! Send amongst them a Messenger of their own, who shall recite unto them Your verses and instruct them in the Book (this Qur'an), and purify them. Verily, You are the Mighty, the Wise."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Our Lord! And raise up in their midst a messenger from among them who shall recite unto them Thy revelations, and shall instruct them in the Scripture and in wisdom and shall make them grow. Lo! Thou, only Thou, art the Mighty, Wise.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Our Lord, send among them a Messenger from them who shall recite to them Your verses and teach them the Book and wisdom, and purify them; You are the Mighty, the Wise'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Our Lord, and send among them a messenger from themselves who will recite to them Your verses and teach them the Book and wisdom and purify them. Indeed, You are the Exalted in Might, the Wise."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Lord, send to them (our descendants) a Messenger of their own who will recite to them Your revelations, teach them the Book, give them wisdom, and purify them. You alone are the Majestic and the Most Wise."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Our Lord! and raise up in them an Apostle from among them who shall recite to them Thy communications and teach them the Book and the wisdom, and purify them; surely Thou art the Mighty, the Wise.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Our Lord, send forth to them a messenger of their own to recite Your revelations to them, to teach them the Scripture and wisdom, and purify them. You are the Mighty, the Wise One."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "Our Lord! send amongst them a Messenger of their own, who shall rehearse Thy Signs to them and instruct them in scripture and wisdom, and sanctify them: For Thou art the Exalted in Might, the Wise."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: When We chose the site of the House for Abraham (We said:) "Associate no one with Me, and clean My House for those who will circumambulate it, stand (in reverence), and bow in homage.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And remember when We showed Ibrahim the right place of the Sacred House (mosque) and commanded that, “Do not ascribe anything as a partner to Me, and keep My House clean for those who encircle it and those who stay in it (for worship) and those who bow and prostrate.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And when We settled for Abraham the place of the House: 'Thou shall not associate with Me anything. And do thou purify My House for those that shall go about it and those that stand, for those that bow and prostrate themselves;

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And recall what time We settled Ibrahim in the place of the House, Saying: associate not thou with Me aught, and purify My House for these who circumambulate and those who stand up and those who bow and make prostration.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And (remember) when We showed Ibrahim (Abraham) the site of the (Sacred) House (the Ka'bah at Makkah) (saying): "Associate not anything (in worship) with Me, [La ilaha ill-Allah (none has the right to be worshipped but Allah Islamic Monotheism], and sanctify My House for those who circumambulate it, and those who stand up for prayer, and those who bow (submit themselves with humility and obedience to Allah), and make prostration (in prayer, etc.);"

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: We showed Abraham the location of the House: “Do not associate anything with Me; and purify My House for those who circle around, and those who stand to pray, and those who kneel and prostrate.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Call to mind when We assigned to Abraham the site of the House (Kabah), directing him: "Do not associate aught with Me" and "Keep My House pure for those who walk around it, and for those who stand and who bow down and who prostrate themselves (in worship),

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And (remember) when We showed Ibrahim the site of the House (saying): "Associate not anything with Me, and sanctify My House for those who circumambulate it, and those who stand up (for prayer), and those who bow and make prostration (in prayer);"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And (remember) when We prepared for Abraham the place of the (holy) House, saying: Ascribe thou no thing as partner unto Me, and purify My House for those who make the round (thereof) and those who stand and those who bow and make prostration.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And when We settled for Abraham (and Ishmael) the place of the Holy Mosque, (We said): 'You shall not associate with Me anything. Purify My House for those who circumambulate it and those who stand, for those who bow and prostrate.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And [mention, O Muhammad], when We designated for Abraham the site of the House, [saying], "Do not associate anything with Me and purify My House for those who perform Tawaf and those who stand [in prayer] and those who bow and prostrate.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: When We prepared for Abraham the place to build the Sacred House, We told him not to consider anything equal to Me and to keep the House clean for those walking around it, those standing, bowing down, and prostrating in prayer.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And when We assigned to Ibrahim the place of the House, saying: Do not associate with Me aught, and purify My House for those who make the circuit and stand to pray and bow and prostrate themselves.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We assigned to Abraham the site of the House, saying, "Do not associate with Me anything and purify My House for those who circumambulate [the Kabah] and those who stand upright, and those who bow and prostrate themselves."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Behold! We gave the site, to Abraham, of the (Sacred) House, (saying): "Associate not anything (in worship) with Me; and sanctify My House for those who compass it round, or stand up, or bow, or prostrate themselves (therein in prayer).

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Announce the Pilgrimage to the people. They will come to you on foot and riding along distant roads on lean and slender beasts,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “And publicly announce the pilgrimage to all people – they will come to you, on foot and on every lean she-camel, coming from every far distant journey.” (The announcement by Prophet Ibrahim reached each and every soul.)

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and proclaim among men the Pilgrimage, and they shall come unto thee on foot and upon every lean beast, they shall come from every deep ravine

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And proclaim thou among mankind the pilgrimage; they shall come unto thee on foot and on any lean mount, coming from every deep defile.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And proclaim to mankind the Hajj (pilgrimage). They will come to you on foot and on every lean camel, they will come from every deep and distant (wide) mountain highway (to perform Hajj).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And announce the pilgrimage to humanity. They will come to you on foot, and on every transport. They will come from every distant point.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: and publicly proclaim Pilgrimage for all mankind so that they come to you on foot and mounted on lean camels from every distant point

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And proclaim to mankind the Hajj (pilgrimage). They will come to you on foot and on every lean camel, they will come from every Fajj `Amiq.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And proclaim unto mankind the pilgrimage. They will come unto thee on foot and on every lean camel; they will come from every deep ravine,

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Proclaim the pilgrimage to the people. They will come to you on foot and on every lean camel, they shall come from every deep ravine;

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And proclaim to the people the Hajj [pilgrimage]; they will come to you on foot and on every lean camel; they will come from every distant pass -

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: (We commanded Abraham), "Call people for hajj - an act of worship accomplished by visiting the sacred sites in Mecca." They will come on foot and on lean camels from all the distant quarters

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And proclaim among men the Pilgrimage: they will come to you on foot and on every lean camel, coming from every remote path,

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Call mankind to the Pilgrimage. They will come to you, on foot, and on every kind of lean camel, by every distant track

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "And proclaim the Pilgrimage among men: they will come to thee on foot and (mounted) on every kind of camel, lean on account of journeys through deep and distant mountain highways;

translation.author: Dr. Muhammad Muhsin
translation.text: Narrated Ibn Abbas: When Abraham had differences with his wife), (because of her jealousy of Hajar, Ishmael's mother), he took Ishmael and his mother and went away. They had a water-skin with them containing some water, Ishmael's mother used to drink water from the water-skin so that her milk would increase for her child. When Abraham reached Mecca, he made her sit under a tree and afterwards returned home. Ishmael's mother followed him, and when they reached Kada', she called him from behind, 'O Abraham! To whom are you leaving us?' He replied, '(I am leaving you) to Allah's (Care).' She said, 'I am satisfied to be with Allah.' She returned to her place and started drinking water from the water-skin, and her milk increased for her child. When the water had all been used up, she said to herself, 'I'd better go and look so that I may see somebody.' She ascended the Safa mountain and looked, hoping to see somebody, but in vain. When she came down to the valley, she ran till she reached the Marwa mountain. She ran to and fro (between the two mountains) many times. They she said to herself, 'i'd better go and see the state of the child,' she went and found it in a state of one on the point of dying. She could not endure to watch it dying and said (to herself), 'If I go and look, I may find somebody.' She went and ascended the Safa mountain and looked for a long while but could not find anybody. Thus she completed seven rounds (of running) between Safa and Marwa. Again she said (to herself), 'I'd better go back and see the state of the child.' But suddenly she heard a voice, and she said to that strange voice, 'Help us if you can offer any help.' Lo! It was Gabriel (who had made the voice). Gabriel hit the earth with his heel like this (Ibn 'Abbas hit the earth with his heel to Illustrate it), and so the water gushed out. Ishmael's mother was astonished and started digging. (Abu Al-Qasim) (i.e. the Prophet) said, "If she had left the water, (flow naturally without her intervention), it would have been flowing on the surface of the earth.") Ishmael's mother started drinking from the water and her milk increased for her child . Afterwards some people of the tribe of Jurhum, while passing through the bottom of the valley, saw some birds, and that astonished them, and they said, 'Birds can only be found at a place where there is water.' They sent a messenger who searched the place and found the water, and returned to inform them about it. Then they all went to her and said, 'O ishmael's mother! Will you allow us to be with you (or dwell with you)?' (And thus they stayed there.) Later on her boy reached the age of puberty and married a lady from them. Then an idea occurred to Abraham which he disclosed to his wife (Sarah), 'I want to call on my dependents I left (at Mecca).' When he went there, he greeted (Ishmael's wife) and said, 'Where is Ishmael?' She replied, 'He has gone out hunting.' Abraham said (to her), 'When he comes, tell him to change the threshold of his gate.' When he came, she told him the same whereupon Ishmael said to her, 'You are the threshold, so go to your family (i.e. you are divorced).' Again Abraham thought of visiting his dependents whom he had left (at Mecca), and he told his wife (Sarah) of his intentions. Abraham came to Ishmael's house and asked. "Where is Ishmael?" Ishmael's wife replied, "He has gone out hunting," and added, "Will you stay (for some time) and have something to eat and drink?' Abraham asked, 'What is your food and what is your drink?' She replied, 'Our food is meat and our drink is water.' He said, 'O Allah! Bless their meals and their drink." Abu Al-Qa-sim (i.e. Prophet) said, "Because of Abraham's invocation there are blessings (in Mecca)." Once more Abraham thought of visiting his family he had left (at Mecca), so he told his wife (Sarah) of his decision. He went and found Ishmael behind the Zam-zam well, mending his arrows. He said, "O Ishmael, Your Lord has ordered me to build a house for Him." Ishmael said, "Obey (the order of) your Lord." Abraham said, "Allah has also ordered me that you should help me therein." Ishmael said, "Then I will do." So, both of them rose and Abraham started building (the Ka'ba) while Ishmael went on handing him the stones, and both of them were saying, "O our Lord ! Accept (this service) from us, Verily, You are the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing." (2.127). When the building became high and the old man (i.e. Abraham) could no longer lift the stones (to such a high position), he stood over the stone of Al-Maqam and Ishmael carried on handing him the stones, and both of them were saying, 'O our Lord! Accept (this service) from us, Verily You are All-Hearing, All-Knowing." (2.127)

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Commemorate Ishmael in the Book. He was true of his promise, and a messenger, a prophet.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And remember Ismail in the Book; he was indeed true to his promise and was a Noble Messenger, a Prophet.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And mention in the Book Ishmael; he was true to his promise, and he was a Messenger, a Prophet.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And mention thou in the Book lsma'i; verily he was true in promise, and was apostle, prophet.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And mention in the Book (the Quran) Isma'il (Ishmael). Verily! He was true to what he promised, and he was a Messenger, (and) a Prophet.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And mention in the Scripture Ishmael. He was true to his promise, and was a messenger, a prophet.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And recite in the Book the account of Ishmael. He was ever true to his promise, and was a Messenger, a Prophet.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And mention in the Book, Isma`il. Verily, he was true to what he promised, and he was a Messenger, (and) a Prophet.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And make mention in the Scripture of Ishmael. Lo! he was a keeper of his promise, and he was a messenger (of Allah), a prophet.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And mention in the Book, Ishmael; he too was true to his promise, a Messenger and a Prophet.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And mention in the Book, Ishmael. Indeed, he was true to his promise, and he was a messenger and a prophet.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Mention in the Book (the Quran) the story of Ishmael; he was true to his promise, a Messengers and a Prophet.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And mention Ismail in the Book; surely he was truthful in (his) promise, and he was an apostle, a prophet.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Tell also of Ishmael in the Book. He was true to his promise and was a messenger and a prophet.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Also mention in the Book (the story of) Isma'il: He was (strictly) true to what he promised, and he was a messenger (and) a prophet.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: He enjoined on his household worship and zakat, and he was obedient to his Lord.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: He used to command his people to offer prayer and give charity, and was liked by his Lord.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: He bade his people to pray and to give the alms, and he was pleasing to his Lord.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And he was wont to command his household to the Prayer and the poor-rate, and he was with his Lord an approved one. *Chapter: 19

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And he used to enjoin on his family and his people As-Salat (the prayers) and the Zakat, and his Lord was pleased with him.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And he used to enjoin on his people prayer and charity, and he was pleasing to his Lord.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: He enjoined his household to observe Prayer and to give Zakah (purifying alms); and his Lord was well pleased with him.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And he would enjoin on his family and his people the Salah and the Zakah, and his Lord was pleased with him.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: He enjoined upon his people worship and almsgiving, and was acceptable in the sight of his Lord.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And he ordered his people to pray and to give charity and his Lord was pleased with him.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And he used to enjoin on his people prayer and zakah and was to his Lord pleasing.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: He would order his people to worship God and pay the religious tax. His Lord was pleased with him.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And he enjoined on his family prayer and almsgiving, and was one in whom his Lord was well pleased.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: He exhorted his people to prayer and almsgiving, and his Lord was pleased with him.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: He used to enjoin on his people Prayer and Charity, and he was most acceptable in the sight of his Lord.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Say: "We believe in God and what has been sent down to us, and what had been revealed to Abraham and Ishmael and Isaac and Jacob and their progeny, and that which was given to Moses and Christ, and to all other prophets by the Lord. We make no distinction among them, and we submit to Him."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Say, “We believe in Allah and what is sent down to us and what was sent down to Ibrahim, and Ismael, and Ishaq, and Yaqub, and to their offspring, and what was bestowed upon Moosa and Eisa (Jesus), and what was bestowed upon other Prophets – from their Lord; we do not make any distinction, in belief, between any of them; and to Allah we have submitted ourselves.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Say you: 'We believe in God, and in that which has been sent down on us and sent down on Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac and Jacob, and the Tribes, and that which was given to Moses and Jesus and the Prophets, of their Lord; we make no division between any of them, and to Him we surrender.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Say: we believe in Allah and that which hath been sent down unto us and that which was sent down unto Ibrahim and Ismai'l and Ishaq and Ya'qub and the tribes, and that which was vouchsafed unto Musa and lsa, and that which was vouchsafed unto the prophets from their Lord; we differentiate not between any of them, and unto Him are submissive.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Say (O Muslims), "We believe in Allah and that which has been sent down to us and that which has been sent down to Ibrahim (Abraham), Isma'il (Ishmael), Ishaque (Isaac), Ya'qub (Jacob), and to Al-Asbat [the twelve sons of Ya'qub (Jacob)], and that which has been given to Musa (Moses) and 'Iesa (Jesus), and that which has been given to the Prophets from their Lord. We make no distinction between any of them, and to Him we have submitted (in Islam)."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Say, “We believe in God; and in what was revealed to us; and in what was revealed to Abraham, and Ishmael, and Isaac, and Jacob, and the Patriarchs; and in what was given to Moses and Jesus; and in what was given to the prophets—from their Lord. We make no distinction between any of them, and to Him we surrender.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: O Muslims, say to them, "We believe in Allah and the Guidance which has been sent down to us and which was sent to Abraham, Ismail, Isaac and Jacob and his descendants and which was given by their Lord to Moses and Jesus and to all other Prophets. We do not discriminate against any of them and we have completely surrendered to Allah as Muslims."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Say (O Muslims): "We believe in Allah and that which has been sent down to us and that which has been sent down to Ibrahim (Abraham), Isma`il (Ishmael), Ishaq (Isaac), Ya`qub (Jacob), and to Al-Asbat (the offspring of the twelve sons of Ya`qub), and that which has been given to Musa (Moses) and `Isa (Jesus), and that which has been given to the Prophets from their Lord. We make no distinction between any of them, and to Him we have submitted (in Islam)."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Say (O Muslims): We believe in Allah and that which is revealed unto us and that which was revealed unto Abraham, and Ishmael, and Isaac, and Jacob, and the tribes, and that which Moses and Jesus received, and that which the prophets received from their Lord. We make no distinction between any of them, and unto Him we have surrendered.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Say: 'We believe in Allah and that which is sent down to us, and in what was sent down to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, and the tribes; to Moses and Jesus and the Prophets from their Lord. We do not differentiate between any of them, and to Him we are submissive (Muslims)'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Say, [O believers], "We have believed in Allah and what has been revealed to us and what has been revealed to Abraham and Ishmael and Isaac and Jacob and the Descendants and what was given to Moses and Jesus and what was given to the prophets from their Lord. We make no distinction between any of them, and we are Muslims [in submission] to Him."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: (Muslims), say, "We believe in God and what He has revealed to us and to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, and their descendants, and what was revealed to Moses, Jesus, and the Prophets from their Lord. We make no distinction among them and to God we have submitted ourselves."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Say: We believe in Allah and (in) that which had been revealed to us, and (in) that which was revealed to Ibrahim and Ismail and Ishaq and Yaqoub and the tribes, and (in) that which was given to Musa and Isa, and (in) that which was given to the prophets from their Lord, we do not make any distinction between any of them, and to Him do we submit.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Say, "We believe in God and what was revealed to us; and what was revealed to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, and their descendants, and what was given to Moses and Jesus and what was given to the [other] prophets by their Lord. We make no distinction between any of them. It is to Him that we surrender ourselves."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Say ye: "We believe in Allah, and the revelation given to us, and to Abraham, Isma'il, Isaac, Jacob, and the Tribes, and that given to Moses and Jesus, and that given to (all) prophets from their Lord: We make no difference between one and another of them: And we bow to Allah (in Islam)."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: (Remember) Ishmael, Edris and Dhu'l-Kifl. They were men of fortitude,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And remember Ismail, and Idrees, and Zul-Kifl; they were all patiently enduring.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And Ishmael, Idris, Dhul Kifl -- each was of the patient,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And Isma'il and ldris and Zul-Kifl! each were of the patient.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And (remember) Isma'il (Ishmael), and Idris (Enoch) and Dhul-Kifl (Isaiah), all were from among As-Sabirin (the patient ones, etc.).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And Ishmael, and Enoch, and Ezekiel; each was one of the steadfast.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And (We bestowed the same favour) upon Ishmael, Idris and Dhu al-Kifl, for they were all steadfast.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And (remember) Isma`il, Idris and Dhul-Kifl: All were from among the patient.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And (mention) Ishmael, and Idris, and Dhu'l-Kifl. All were of the steadfast.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And Ishmael, Idris, and ThulKifl (the son of Job) each were patient.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And [mention] Ishmael and Idrees and Dhul-Kifl; all were of the patient.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Ismael, Idris and Dhul Kifl all were people of great patience.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And Ismail and Idris and Zulkifl; all were of the patient ones;

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Remember Ishmael and Idris and Dhul Kifl: they were all patient and steadfast.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And (remember) Isma'il, Idris, and Zul-kifl, all (men) of constancy and patience;

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And they were admitted to Our grace. Verily they were among the doers of good.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We admitted them into Our mercy; indeed they are among those who deserve Our proximity.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and We admitted them into Our mercy; they were of the righteous.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And We caused them to enter into our mercy, verily they are of the righteous.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And We admitted them to Our Mercy. Verily, they were of the righteous.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And We admitted them into Our mercy. They were among the righteous.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And We admitted them into Our mercy, for they were of the righteous.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And We admitted them to Our mercy. Verily, they were of the righteous.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And We brought them in unto Our mercy. Lo! they are among the righteous.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We admitted them to Our Mercy, for they were of the righteous.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We admitted them into Our mercy. Indeed, they were of the righteous.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We encompassed them in Our mercy; they were righteous people.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And We caused them to enter into Our mercy, surely they were of the good ones.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We admitted them to Our mercy. They were all righteous men.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: We admitted them to Our mercy: for they were of the righteous ones.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Our angels came to Abraham with good news, and said: "Peace on you. "Peace on you too," said Abraham, and hastened to bring a roasted calf.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And indeed Our angels came to Ibrahim with glad tidings – they said, “Peace”; he answered, “Peace” and without delay brought a roasted calf.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Our messengers came to Abraham with the good tidings; they said, 'Peace!' 'Peace,' he said; and presently he brought a roasted calf.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And assuredly Our messengers came unto Ibrahim with the glad tidings. The said: peace! He said: peace! And he tarried not till he brougt a calf roasted.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And verily, there came Our Messengers to Ibrahim (Abraham) with glad tidings. They said: Salam (greetings or peace!) He answered, Salam (greetings or peace!) and he hastened to entertain them with a roasted calf.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Our messengers came to Abraham with good news. They said, “Peace.” He said, “Peace.” Soon after, he came with a roasted calf.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Indeed Our messengers came to Abraham, bearing glad tidings. They greeted him with 'peace', and Abraham answered back to them 'peace', and hurriedly brought to them a roasted calf.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And verily, there came Our messengers to Ibrahim with the glad tidings. They said: "Salaman (greetings or peace!)" He answered, "Salamun (greetings or peace!)," and he hastened to entertain them with a roasted calf.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And Our messengers cam unto Abraham with good news. They said: Peace! He answered: Peace! and delayed not to bring a roasted calf.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Our Messengers came to Abraham with glad tidings. They said: 'Peace' He replied 'Peace' and in a while brought them a roasted calf.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And certainly did Our messengers come to Abraham with good tidings; they said, "Peace." He said, "Peace," and did not delay in bringing [them] a roasted calf.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Our Messengers came to Abraham with glad news. They said, "Peace be with you." He replied similarly. After a short time he presented them with a roasted calf

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And certainly Our apostles came to Ibrahim with good news. They said: Peace. Peace, said he, and he made no delay in bringing a roasted calf.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Our messengers came to Abraham with good news and greeted him with, "Peace." He too said, "Peace be on you," and hastened to bring a roasted calf.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: There came Our messengers to Abraham with glad tidings. They said, "Peace!" He answered, "Peace!" and hastened to entertain them with a roasted calf.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: When they did not stretch their hands towards it he became suspicious and afraid of them. They said: "Do not be afraid. We have been sent to the people of Lot."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And when he saw their hands not reaching towards it, he thought they were pretending and inwardly started fearing them; they said, “Do not be afraid – we are sent to the people of Lut.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And when he saw their hands not reaching towards it, he was suspicious of them and conceived a fear of them. They said, 'Fear not; we have been sent to the people of Lot.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And when he beheld that their hands reached it not he misliked them, and ccnceived a fear of them. They said fear not verily we are sent unto the people of Lut.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: But when he saw their hands went not towards it (the meal), he felt some mistrust of them, and conceived a fear of them. They said: "Fear not, we have been sent against the people of Lout (Lot)."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: But when he saw their hands not reaching towards it, he became suspicious of them, and conceived a fear of them. They said, “Do not fear, we were sent to the people of Lot.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: When he perceived that their hands could not reach it, he mistrusted them, and felt afraid of them. They said: 'Do not be afraid. We have been sent to the people of Lot.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: But when he saw their hands went not towards it (the meal), he mistrusted them, and conceived a fear of them. They said: "Fear not, we have been sent against the people of Lut."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And when he saw their hands reached not to it, he mistrusted them and conceived a fear of them. They said: Fear not! Lo! we are sent unto the folk of Lot.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: But when he saw that their hands did not reach towards it, he was wary and became fearful of them, but they said: 'Do not be afraid. We are sent to thenation of Lot'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: But when he saw their hands not reaching for it, he distrusted them and felt from them apprehension. They said, "Fear not. We have been sent to the people of Lot."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: but when he saw that their hands did not reach out for it, he could not know who they were and became afraid of them. They said, "Do not be afraid; we are God's angelic Messengers sent to the people of Lot."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: But when he saw that their hands were not extended towards it, he deemed them strange and conceived fear of them. They said: Fear not, surely we are sent to Lut's people.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: But when he saw that they made no move to eat, he found this strange and became afraid of them. They said, "Do not be afraid, for we have been sent to the people of Lot."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: But when he saw their hands went not towards the (meal), he felt some mistrust of them, and conceived a fear of them. They said: "Fear not: We have been sent against the people of Lut."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: His wife who stood near, laughed as We gave her the good news of Isaac, and after Isaac of Jacob.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And his wife was standing by and she started laughing*, so We gave her glad tidings regarding Ishaq, and following Ishaq, regarding Yaqub.** (* She was glad that the disbelieving people of Lut would be destroyed.** The birth of these two.)

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And his wife was standing by; she laughed, therefore We gave her the glad tidings of Isaac, and, after Isaac, of Jacob.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And his wife was standing. she laughed. Then We gave her the glad tidings of Is'haq, and after Is'haq, Ya'qub.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And his wife was standing (there), and she laughed (either, because the Messengers did not eat their food or for being glad for the destruction of the people of Lout (Lot). But We gave her glad tidings of Ishaque (Isaac), and after him, of Ya'qub (Jacob).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: His wife was standing by, so she laughed. And We gave her good news of Isaac; and after Isaac, Jacob.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And Abraham's wife was standing by and on hearing this she laughed. And We gave her the good news of (the birth of) Isaac, and after Isaac, of Jacob.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And his wife was standing (there), and she laughed. But We gave her glad tidings of Ishaq, and after Ishaq, of Ya`qub.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And his wife, standing by laughed when We gave her good tidings (of the birth) of Isaac, and, after Isaac, of Jacob.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: His wife (Sarah), who was standing nearby, laughed. Thereupon, We gave her the good news of Isaac, and after Isaac of Jacob.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And his Wife was standing, and she smiled. Then We gave her good tidings of Isaac and after Isaac, Jacob.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: His wife who was standing nearby, smiled and so We gave her the glad news that she would give birth to Isaac who would have a son, Jacob.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And his wife was standing (by), so she laughed, then We gave her the good news of Ishaq and after Ishaq of (a son's son) Yaqoub.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: His wife, who was standing nearby, laughed when We gave her the good news of Isaac, and after Isaac, Jacob.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And his wife was standing (there), and she laughed: But we gave her glad tidings of Isaac, and after him, of Jacob.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: She said: "Woe betide me! Will I give birth when I am old and this my husband be aged? This is indeed surprising!"

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: She said, “Oh woe to me – will I bear a child whereas I am an old woman, and this my husband, is an old man? This is something really extraordinary.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: She said, 'Woe is me! Shall I bear, being an old woman, and this my husband is an old man? This assuredly is a strange thing.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: She said: Oh for me shall bring forth when I am old, and this my husband is advanced in years! Verily a marvellous thing is this!

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: She said (in astonishment): "Woe unto me! Shall I bear a child while I am an old woman, and here is my husband, an old man? Verily! This is a strange thing!"

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: She said, “Alas for me. Shall I give birth, when I am an old woman, and this, my husband, is an old man? This is truly a strange thing.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: She said: 'Woe is me! Shall I bear a child now that I am an old woman and my husband is well advanced in years. This is indeed strange!'

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: She said (in astonishment): "Woe unto me! Shall I bear a child while I am an old woman, and here is my husband an old man Verily, this is a strange thing!"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: She said: Oh woe is me! Shall I bear a child when I am an old woman, and this my husband is an old man? Lo! this is a strange thing!

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: She replied: 'Alas for me! Shall I bear (a child) when I am old woman and my husband is advanced in years? This is indeed a strange thing'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: She said, "Woe to me! Shall I give birth while I am an old woman and this, my husband, is an old man? Indeed, this is an amazing thing!"

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: She said, "Woe is me! How can I have a baby when I am barren and my husband is very old? This is certainly strange".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: She said: O wonder! shall I bear a son when I am an extremely old woman and this my husband an extremely old man? Most surely this is a wonderful thing.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: She said, "Alas! Shall I bear a child in this old age, while my husband here is also old? This is indeed a strange thing!"

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: She said: "Alas for me! shall I bear a child, seeing I am an old woman, and my husband here is an old man? That would indeed be a wonderful thing!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: "Why are you surprised at the command of God? God's mercy and blessings be upon you, O members of this household," they said. "Verily He is worthy of praise and glory."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: The angels said, “Do you wonder at the command of Allah? Allah’s mercy and His blessings be upon you, O people of this house; indeed He only is Most Praiseworthy, Most Honourable.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: They said, 'What, dost thou marvel at God's command? The mercy of God and His blessings be upon you, O people of the House! Surely He is All-laudable, All-glorious.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: They said marvellest thou at the decree of Allah? Mercy of Allah and His blessings be upon you, people of the house, verily He is Praiseworthy, Glorious.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: They said: "Do you wonder at the Decree of Allah? The Mercy of Allah and His Blessings be on you, O the family [of Ibrahim (Abraham)]. Surely, He (Allah) is All-Praiseworthy, All-Glorious."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: They said, “Do you marvel at the decree of God? The mercy and blessings of God are upon you, O people of the house. He is Praiseworthy and Glorious.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: They said: 'Do you wonder at Allah's decree? Allah's mercy and His blessings be upon you, O people of the house. Surely, He is Praiseworthy, Glorious.'

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: They said: "Do you wonder at the decree of Allah The mercy of Allah and His blessings be on you, O the family [of Ibrahim]. Surely, He (Allah) is All-Praiseworthy, All-Glorious."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: They said: Wonderest thou at the commandment of Allah? The mercy of Allah and His blessings be upon you, O people of the house! Lo! He is Owner of Praise, Owner of Glory!

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: They said: 'What, do you marvel at the command of Allah? The Mercy of Allah and His Blessings be upon you, O people of the House. Indeed, He isPraised, Exalted'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: They said, "Are you amazed at the decree of Allah? May the mercy of Allah and His blessings be upon you, people of the house. Indeed, He is Praiseworthy and Honorable."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: They replied, "Would you be surprised at God's decree? People of the house, may God's mercy and blessings be with you. God is Appreciative and Glorious."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: They said: Do you wonder at Allah's bidding? The mercy of Allah and His blessings are on you, O people of the house, surely He is Praised, Glorious.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: They said, "Are you astonished at God's command? May the mercy of God and His blessings be upon you, O people of this house. Surely, He is praiseworthy and glorious."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: They said: "Dost thou wonder at Allah's decree? The grace of Allah and His blessings on you, o ye people of the house! for He is indeed worthy of all praise, full of all glory!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: So We gave him the good news of Isaac, apostle, who is among the righteous.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We gave him the glad tidings of Ishaq, a Herald of the Hidden, from among those who deserve Our proximity.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Then We gave him the good tidings of Isaac, a Prophet, one of the righteous.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And We gave him the glad tidings of Is-haq, a prophet, and of the righteous.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And We gave him the glad tidings of Ishaque (Isaac) a Prophet from the righteous.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And We gave him good news of Isaac, a prophet, one of the righteous.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And We gave him the good news of Isaac, a Prophet and among the righteous ones.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And We gave him the glad tidings of Ishaq -- a Prophet from the righteous.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And we gave him tidings of the birth of Isaac, a prophet of the righteous.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Then, We gave him the glad tidings of Isaac, a Prophet, one of the righteous,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We gave him good tidings of Isaac, a prophet from among the righteous.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We gave him the glad news of the birth of Isaac, one of the righteous Prophets.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And We gave him the good news of Ishaq, a prophet among the good ones.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We gave Abraham the good news of Isaac, a prophet and a righteous man,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And We gave him the good news of Isaac - a prophet,- one of the Righteous.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And We blessed him and Isaac. Among their descendants are some who do good, but some who wrong themselves.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We sent blessings upon him and Ishaq; and among their descendants – some who do good deeds, and some who clearly wrong themselves.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And We blessed him, and Isaac; and of their seed some are good-doers, and some manifest self-wrongers.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And We blessed him and Is-haq; and of their offspring are some well-doers and some who wrong themselves manifestly.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: We blessed him and Ishaque (Isaac), and of their progeny are (some) that do right, and some that plainly wrong themselves.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And We blessed him, and Isaac. But among their descendants are some who are righteous, and some who are clearly unjust to themselves.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And We blessed him and Isaac. Among the offspring of the two some did good and some plainly wronged themselves.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: We blessed him and Ishaq. And of their progeny are (some) that do right, and some that plainly wrong themselves.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And We blessed him and Isaac. And of their seed are some who do good, and some who plainly wrong themselves.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: and We blessed him and Isaac, and from their offspring are some gooddoers, and others who clearly wronged themselves.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We blessed him and Isaac. But among their descendants is the doer of good and the clearly unjust to himself.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We had blessed him and Isaac. Some of their offspring were righteous and others were openly unjust to themselves.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And We showered Our blessings on him and on Ishaq; and of their offspring are the doers of good, and (also) those who are clearly unjust to their own souls.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: and blessed him and Isaac too: some of their offspring were good, but some clearly sinned against their souls.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: We blessed him and Isaac: but of their progeny are (some) that do right, and (some) that obviously do wrong, to their own souls.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And We bestowed on him Isaac, and Jacob as an additional gift, and made them righteous.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We bestowed him Ishaq, and Yaqub the grandson; and We made all of them worthy of Our proximity.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And We gave him Isaac and Jacob in superfluity, and every one made We righteous

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And We bestowed upon him ls-haq and Ya qub as a grandson; and each one We made righteous.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And We bestowed upon him Ishaque (Isaac), and (a grandson) Ya'qub (Jacob). Each one We made righteous.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And We granted him Isaac and Jacob as a gift; and each We made righteous.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And We bestowed upon him Isaac and Jacob as an additional gift, making each of them righteous.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And We bestowed upon him Ishaq, and Ya`qub Nafilatan. Each one We made righteous.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And We bestowed upon him Isaac, and Jacob as a grandson. Each of them We made righteous.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We gave him, in excess, Isaac, and Jacob (for a grandson); and We made each righteous

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We gave him Isaac and Jacob in addition, and all [of them] We made righteous.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We granted him Isaac and Jacob as a gift and helped both of them to become righteous people.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And We gave him Ishaq and Yaqoub, a son's son, and We made (them) all good.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We bestowed Isaac and then Jacob on him as an additional boon and We made all of them righteous.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And We bestowed on him Isaac and, as an additional gift, (a grandson), Jacob, and We made righteous men of every one (of them).

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Thus, when he left them and the (idols) they worshipped, We bestowed on him Isaac and Jacob, and made each of them a prophet,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: So when he had separated from them and what they worshipped other than Allah, We bestowed him Ishaq and Yaqub; and We made each of them a Herald of the Hidden.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: So, when he went apart from them and that they were serving, apart from God, We gave him Isaac and Jacob, and each We made a Prophet;

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Then when he had renounced them and that which they worshipped beside Allah, We best owed on him Ishaq and Yaqub and each one We made a prophet!

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: So when he had turned away from them and from those whom they worshipped besides Allah, We gave him Ishaque (Isaac) and Ya'qub (Jacob), and each one of them We made a Prophet.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: When he withdrew from them, and from what they worship besides God, We granted him Isaac and Jacob. And each We made a prophet.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Thereupon Abraham dissociated himself from his people and the deities they worshipped instead of Allah, and We bestowed upon him Isaac and Jacob and made each of them a Prophet;

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: So when he had turned away from them and from those whom they worshipped besides Allah, We gave him Ishaq and Ya`qub, and each one of them We made a Prophet.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: So, when he had withdrawn from them and that which they were worshipping beside Allah, We gave him Isaac and Jacob. Each of them We made a prophet.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: So when he turned away from them and those they worshipped, other than Allah, We gave him Isaac and Jacob. Each of them We made a Prophet,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: So when he had left them and those they worshipped other than Allah, We gave him Isaac and Jacob, and each [of them] We made a prophet.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: When (Abraham) rejected his people and what they worshipped instead of God, We gave him Isaac and Jacob and made both of them Prophets.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: So when he withdrew from them and what they worshipped besides Allah, We gave to him Ishaq and Yaqoub, and each one of them We made a prophet.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: So when he had separated himself from them and from what they worshipped besides God, We bestowed on him Isaac and Jacob, and We made each of them a prophet.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: When he had turned away from them and from those whom they worshipped besides Allah, We bestowed on him Isaac and Jacob, and each one of them We made a prophet.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And bestowed on them some of Our blessings, and gave them high renown.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We gave them Our mercy, and assigned for them a true and high repute.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and We gave them of Our mercy, and We appointed unto them a tongue of truthfulness, sublime.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And We bestowed on them of Our mercy, and We made for them a renown lofty.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And We gave them of Our Mercy (a good provision in plenty), and We granted them honour on the tongues (of all the nations, i.e. everybody remembers them with a good praise).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And We gave them freely of Our mercy, and gave them a noble reputation of truth.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: and We bestowed on them Our mercy, and granted them a truly lofty renown.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And We gave them of Our mercy, and We granted them Sidqin `Aliyyan on the tongues.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And we gave them of Our mercy, and assigned to them a high and true renown.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: and We gave them of Our Mercy and We appointed to them a truthful, highly sincere tongue,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We gave them of Our mercy, and we made for them a reputation of high honor.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We granted them Our blessing and high renown.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And We granted to them of Our mercy, and We left (behind them) a truthful mention of eminence for them.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We granted them Our mercy and bestowed on them true and high renown.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And We bestowed of Our Mercy on them, and We granted them lofty honour on the tongue of truth.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: So We bestowed on him Isaac and Jacob and gave his progeny prophethood and scripture, and rewarded him in this world, and in the next he will be among the upright.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We bestowed Ishaq and Yaqub to him, and kept the Prophethood and the Book among his descendants, and We gave him his reward in the world; and in the Hereafter he is indeed among those who deserve Our proximity.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And We gave him Isaac and Jacob, and We appointed the Prophecy and the Book to be among his seed; We gave him his wage in this world, and in the world to come he shall be among the righteous.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And We bestowed on him Is-haq and Y'aqub, and We placed among his posterity prophethood and the Book, and We vouchsafed unto him his hire in the World, and verily in the Hereafter he shall be of the righteous.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And We bestowed on him [Ibrahim (Abraham)], Ishaque (Isaac) and Ya'qub (Jacob), and ordained among his offspring Prophethood and the Book [i.e. the Taurat (Torah) (to Musa - Moses), the Injeel (Gospel) (to 'Iesa - Jesus), the Quran (to Muhammad SAW), all from the offspring of Ibrahim (Abraham)], and We granted him his reward in this world, and verily, in the Hereafter he is indeed among the righteous.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And We granted him Isaac and Jacob, and conferred on his descendants the Prophethood and the Book, and gave him his reward in this life; and in the Hereafter he will be among the upright.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: and We bestowed upon him (offspring like) Isaac and Jacob, and bestowed prophethood and the Book on his descendants and granted him his reward in this world; he will certainly be among the righteous in the Hereafter.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And We bestowed on him, Ishaq and Ya`qub, and We ordained among his offspring prophethood and the Book, and We granted him his reward in this world; and verily, in the Hereafter he is indeed among the righteous.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And We bestowed on him Isaac and Jacob, and We established the prophethood and the Scripture among his seed, and We gave him his reward in the world, and lo! in the Hereafter he verily is among the righteous.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And, We gave him (Abraham), Isaac and Jacob and appointed on his descendants the Prophethood and the Book. We gave him his wage in this world, and in the Everlasting Life he shall be among the righteous.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We gave to Him Isaac and Jacob and placed in his descendants prophethood and scripture. And We gave him his reward in this world, and indeed, he is in the Hereafter among the righteous.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We granted Isaac and Jacob to Abraham and We bestowed upon his offspring, prophethood and the Book. We gave him his reward in this world and in the next life, he will be among the pious ones.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And We granted him Ishaq and Yaqoub, and caused the prophethood and the book to remain in his seed, and We gave him his reward in this world, and in the hereafter he will most surely be among the good.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We gave him Isaac and Jacob and granted prophethood and the Book to his descendants. We gave him his reward in this life and in the Hereafter he shall dwell among the righteous.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And We gave (Abraham) Isaac and Jacob, and ordained among his progeny Prophethood and Revelation, and We granted him his reward in this life; and he was in the Hereafter (of the company) of the Righteous.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Who will turn away from the creed of Abraham but one dull of soul? We made him the chosen one here in the world, and one of the best in the world to come,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And who will renounce the religion of Ibrahim except him who is a fool at heart? We indeed chose him (Ibrahim) in this world; and indeed in the Hereafter he is among those worthy of being closest to Us.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Who therefore shrinks from the religion of Abraham, except he be foolish-minded? Indeed, We chose him in the present world, and in the world to come he shall be among the righteous.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And who shall be averse from the faith of Ibrahim, save one who befooleth his soul! And assuredly We chose him in this World, and verily he in the Hereafter shall be of the righteous.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And who turns away from the religion of Ibrahim (Abraham) (i.e. Islamic Monotheism) except him who befools himself? Truly, We chose him in this world and verily, in the Hereafter he will be among the righteous.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Who would forsake the religion of Abraham, except he who fools himself? We chose him in this world, and in the Hereafter he will be among the righteous.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Now, who else can have aversion to the way of Abraham but the one who has debased himself with folly and ignorance? Abraham was the man whom We chose for Our service in this world, and in the Next World he shall be among the righteous.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And who turns away from the religion of Ibrahim (i.e. Islamic Monotheism) except him who fools himself Truly, We chose him in this world and verily, in the Hereafter he will be among the righteous.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And who forsaketh the religion of Abraham save him who befooleth himself? Verily We chose him in the world, and lo! in the Hereafter he is among the righteous.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And who has no desire for the religion of Abraham, except he who fooled himself? We chose him in this world, and in the Everlasting world, he shall be among the righteous.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And who would be averse to the religion of Abraham except one who makes a fool of himself. And We had chosen him in this world, and indeed he, in the Hereafter, will be among the righteous.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: No one turns away from Abraham's Tradition except one who makes a fool of himself. To Abraham We have granted distinction in this world and in the life hereafter he will be among the righteous ones.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And who forsakes the religion of Ibrahim but he who makes himself a fool, and most certainly We chose him in this world, and in the hereafter he is most surely among the righteous.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Who but a foolish man would turn away from the religion of Abraham? We chose him in this world, and in the Hereafter he shall be among the righteous.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And who turns away from the religion of Abraham but such as debase their souls with folly? Him We chose and rendered pure in this world: And he will be in the Hereafter in the ranks of the Righteous.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: (For) when his Lord said to him: "Obey," he replied: "I submit to the Lord of all the worlds."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: When his Lord said to him, “Submit”, he said, “I have submitted to the Lord Of The Creation.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: When his Lord said to him, 'Surrender,' he said, 'I have surrendered me to the Lord of all Being.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Recall what time his Lord said unto him: submit, he said: I submit to the Lord of the Worlds.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: When his Lord said to him, "Submit (i.e. be a Muslim)!" He said, "I have submitted myself (as a Muslim) to the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists)."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: When his Lord said to him, “Submit!” He said, “I have submitted to the Lord of the Worlds.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: When his Lord said to him, "Surrender," he promptly responded, "I have surrendered to the Lord of the Universe (and become a Muslim)."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: When his Lord said to him, "Submit (i.e. be a Muslim)!" He said, "I have submitted myself (as a Muslim) to the Lord of the `Alamin (mankind, Jinn and all that exists)."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: When his Lord said unto him: Surrender! he said: I have surrendered to the Lord of the Worlds.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: When his Lord said to him: 'Submit' he answered: 'I have submitted to the Lord of the Worlds'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: When his Lord said to him, "Submit", he said "I have submitted [in Islam] to the Lord of the worlds."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: When God commanded Abraham to submit, he replied, "I have submitted myself to the Will of the Lord of the universe."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: When his Lord said to him, Be a Muslim, he said: I submit myself to the Lord of the worlds.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: When his Lord said to him, "Surrender!" he responded, "I have surrendered to the Lord of the Universe,"

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Behold! his Lord said to him: "Bow (thy will to Me):" He said: "I bow (my will) to the Lord and Cherisher of the Universe."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And Abraham left this legacy to his sons, and to Jacob, and said: "O my sons, God has chosen this as the faith for you. Do not die but as those who have submitted (to God)."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And Ibrahim willed the same religion upon his sons, and also did Yaqub (Jacob); (saying), “O my sons – indeed Allah has chosen this religion for you; therefore do not die except as Muslims (those who submit to Him).”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And Abraham charged his sons with this and Jacob likewise: 'My sons, God has chosen for you the religion; see that you die not save in surrender.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And Ibrahim enjoined his sons the same and did Ya'qub also, saying: O my sons! verily Allah hath chosen for you the religion, so die not except ye be Muslims.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And this (submission to Allah, Islam) was enjoined by Ibrahim (Abraham) upon his sons and by Ya'qub (Jacob), (saying), "O my sons! Allah has chosen for you the (true) religion, then die not except in the Faith of Islam (as Muslims - Islamic Monotheism)."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And Abraham exhorted his sons, and Jacob, “O my sons, God has chosen this religion for you, so do not die unless you have submitted.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: He also enjoined on his children to follow the same way. Jacob also did the same and his last will to his sons was, "O my children, Allah has chosen the same way of life for you. Hence remain Muslims up to your last breath."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And this (submission to Allah, Islam) was enjoined by Ibrahim (Abraham) upon his sons and by Ya`qub (Jacob) (saying), "O my sons! Allah has chosen for you the (true) religion, then die not except as Muslims."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: The same did Abraham enjoin upon his sons, and also Jacob, (saying): O my sons! Lo! Allah hath chosen for you the (true) religion; therefore die not save as men who have surrendered (unto Him).

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Abraham charged his children with this, and so did Jacob, saying: 'My sons, Allah has chosen for you the religion. Do not die except being submissive (Muslims)'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And Abraham instructed his sons [to do the same] and [so did] Jacob, [saying], "O my sons, indeed Allah has chosen for you this religion, so do not die except while you are Muslims."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Abraham left this legacy to his sons and, in turn, so did Jacob saying, "God has chosen this religion for you. You must not leave this world unless you are a Muslim (submitted to the will of the Lord of the Universe)."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And the same did Ibrahim enjoin on his sons and (so did) Yaqoub. O my sons! surely Allah has chosen for you (this) faith, therefore die not unless you are Muslims.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: and Abraham enjoined his sons to do the same, as did Jacob, "My sons, God has chosen this religion for you; so do not let death overtake you, except when you are in a state of submission."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And this was the legacy that Abraham left to his sons, and so did Jacob; "Oh my sons! Allah hath chosen the Faith for you; then die not except in the Faith of Islam."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Were you present at the hour of Jacob's death? "What will you worship after me?" he asked his sons, and they answered: "We shall worship your God and the God of your fathers, of Abraham and Ishmael and Isaac, the one and only God; and to Him we submit."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: In fact, some of you yourselves were present when death approached Yaqub and when he said to his sons, “What will you worship after me?” They said, “We shall worship Him Who is your God, and is the God of your fathers, Ibrahim and Ismail and Ishaq (Isaac) – the One God; and to Him we have submitted ourselves.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Why, were you witnesses, when death came to Jacob? When he said to his sons, 'What will you serve after me?' They said, 'We will serve thy God and the God of thy fathers Abraham, Ishmael and Isaac, One God; to Him we surrender.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Were ye witnesses when death presented itself to Ya'qub, what time he said unto his sons: what will ye worship after me! They said: we shall worship the God the God of thy fathers, Ibrahim and Ismai'l and Ishaq, and unto Him we are submissive.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Or were you witnesses when death approached Ya'qub (Jacob)? When he said unto his sons, "What will you worship after me?" They said, "We shall worship your Ilah (God - Allah), the Ilah (God) of your fathers, Ibrahim (Abraham), Isma'il (Ishmael), Ishaque (Isaac), One Ilah (God), and to Him we submit (in Islam)."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Or were you witnesses when death approached Jacob, and he said to his sons, “What will you worship after Me?” They said, “We will worship your God, and the God of your fathers, Abraham, Ishmael, and Isaac; One God; and to Him we submit.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Were you present at the time when Jacob was on the point of death? He asked his children, "Whom will you worship after me?" They all answered, "We will worship the same One Allah Whom you, your forefathers Abraham. Ismail and Isaac acknowledged as their Allah and to Him we all surrender as Muslims."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Or were you witnesses when death approached Ya`qub (Jacob) When he said unto his sons, "What will you worship after me" They said, "We shall worship your AIlah (God ـ Allah) the Ilah of your fathers, Ibrahim (Abraham), Isma`il (Ishmael), Ishaq (Isaac), One Ilah, and to Him we submit (in Islam)."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Or were ye present when death came to Jacob, when he said unto his sons: What will ye worship after me? They said: We shall worship thy god, the god of thy fathers, Abraham and Ishmael and Isaac, One Allah, and unto Him we have surrendered.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Or, were you witnesses when death came to Jacob! He said to his children: 'What will you worship after me' They replied: 'We will worship your God and the God of your forefathers, Abraham, Ishmael, and Isaac, the One God. To Him, we are submissive'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Or were you witnesses when death approached Jacob, when he said to his sons, "What will you worship after me?" They said, "We will worship your God and the God of your fathers, Abraham and Ishmael and Isaac - one God. And we are Muslims [in submission] to Him."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Were you (believers) there when death approached Jacob? When he asked his sons, "Whom will you worship after my death?" They replied, "We will worship your Lord, the Lord of your fathers, Abraham, Ishmael, and Isaac. He is the only Lord, and to Him we have submitted ourselves."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Nay! were you witnesses when death visited Yaqoub, when he said to his sons: What will you serve after me? They said: We will serve your God and the God of your fathers, Ibrahim and Ismail and Ishaq, one God only, and to Him do we submit.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Were you present when Jacob faced the hour of death and he asked his sons, "Who will you worship after me?" They answered, "We will worship your God and the God of your fathers, Abraham and Ishmael and Isaac: the one God; we have submitted ourselves to Him."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Were ye witnesses when death appeared before Jacob? Behold, he said to his sons: "What will ye worship after me?" They said: "We shall worship Thy god and the god of thy fathers, of Abraham, Isma'il and Isaac,- the one (True) Allah: To Him we bow (in Islam)."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Neither was Abraham a Jew nor a Christian, but upright and obedient, and not an idolater.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Ibrahim was neither a Jew nor a Christian; but he was a Muslim, free from all falsehood; and was not of the polytheists.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: No; Abraham in truth was not a Jew, neither a Christian; but he was a Muslim and one pure of faith; certainly he was never of the idolaters.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Ibrahim was not a Jew, nor a Nazarene, but he was an upright Muslim, nor was he of the associators.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Ibrahim (Abraham) was neither a Jew nor a Christian, but he was a true Muslim Hanifa (Islamic Monotheism - to worship none but Allah Alone) and he was not of Al-Mushrikun (See V. 2:105).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Abraham was neither a Jew nor a Christian, but he was a Monotheist, a Muslim. And he was not of the Polytheists.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Abraham was neither a Jew nor a Christian; he was a Muslim, wholly devoted to God. And he certainly was not amongst those who associate others with Allah in His divinity.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Ibrahim was neither a Jew nor a Christian, but he was a true Muslim Hanifa and he was not of the Mushrikin

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Abraham was not a Jew, nor yet a Christian; but he was an upright man who had surrendered (to Allah), and he was not of the idolaters.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: No, Abraham was neither a Jew nor a Nazarene. He was of pure faith, a submitter (Muslim). He was never of the idolaters.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Abraham was neither a Jew nor a Christian, but he was one inclining toward truth, a Muslim [submitting to Allah]. And he was not of the polytheists.
